arabic: وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ
translation: Whoever fears Allah, He will make for him a way out, and provide from where he does not expect.
tafsir: This ayah promises that taqwa (God-consciousness) leads to divine provision and relief from difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your {weakest_area} needs attention. Taqwa — fear and awareness of Allah — is the key. Prioritize your connection with Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this ayah with someone anxious about their future. Allah's provision comes from where we least expect.

arabic: فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا
translation: For indeed, with hardship comes ease. Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
tafsir: Allah promises ease after every difficulty — mentioned twice for emphasis. Hardship is temporary; ease is guaranteed.
reflectionTemplate: You are going through difficulty, but Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. Hold on.
dawahTemplate: Someone around you is struggling. Remind them: ease is not just possible — it is promised by Allah.

arabic: إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلَافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لَآيَاتٍ لِّأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ
translation: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and earth, and the alternation of night and day, are signs for those of understanding.
tafsir: This ayah invites reflection on creation — finding Allah in the patterns of the universe is the essence of taqwa.
reflectionTemplate: Your time barakah is low and your taqwa was distracted. This ayah invites you to LOOK — to find Allah in the patterns of your day.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels life is random. The signs are everywhere — they just need someone to point at them.

arabic: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا
translation: Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear.
tafsir: Every test is calibrated to your capacity. What you face, you can handle — with Allah's help.
reflectionTemplate: Whatever you are facing, Allah knows you can handle it. He does not burden you beyond your capacity.
dawahTemplate: Remind someone overwhelmed by life: Allah, who created them, knows exactly what they can bear.

arabic: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ وَاشْكُرُوا لِي وَلَا تَكْفُرُونِ
translation: So remember Me; I will remember you. And be grateful to Me and do not deny Me.
tafsir: Dhikr (remembrance of Allah) is a two-way connection — when you remember Him, He remembers you with His mercy and blessings.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is low because you have forgotten to remember Him. Start with even one dhikr — He will remember you back.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels disconnected from Allah. The connection starts with just one moment of remembrance.

arabic: قُلْ يَا عِبَادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا مِن رَّحْمَةِ اللَّهِ
translation: Say: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah.
tafsir: Allah addresses the sinners directly — His mercy is greater than any sin. Despair is never the answer.
reflectionTemplate: You may feel you have gone too far, but Allah's mercy is infinite. Do not despair — turn back to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they are too sinful for Allah's mercy. He calls them 'My servants' — not sinners.

arabic: وَمَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ وَلَلْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لَّكَ مِنَ الْأُولَىٰ
translation: Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor has He become displeased. And the Hereafter is better for you than the first life.
tafsir: Revealed during a period of silence in revelation — Allah reassures the Prophet ﷺ that He has not abandoned him.
reflectionTemplate: If you feel Allah has forgotten you, He has not. The best is yet to come — in this life and the next.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone going through a silent, difficult season. Allah has not forsaken them.

arabic: أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ
translation: Verily, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest.
tafsir: True peace comes not from circumstances but from connection with the Creator. Dhikr is the medicine for restless hearts.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless because it is disconnected from its source of peace. Return to dhikr — even quietly.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone searching for inner peace. The answer has been there all along — remembrance of Allah.

arabic: قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
translation: Say: Indeed, my prayer, my rites of sacrifice, my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds.
tafsir: This is the ultimate declaration of purpose — everything we do is for Allah. Salah is the anchor of this purpose.
reflectionTemplate: Your prayer consistency reflects your connection to purpose. Every salah is a meeting with the One who created you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who asks why you pray. Your salah is not a ritual — it is a declaration of purpose.

arabic: اتْلُ مَا أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ إِنَّ الصَّلَاةَ تَنْهَىٰ عَنِ الْفَحْشَاءِ وَالْمُنكَرِ
translation: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book and establish prayer. Indeed, prayer prohibits immorality and wrongdoing.
tafsir: Salah is not just worship — it is a shield against sin. When prayer is strong, character is protected.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is low and your salah needs focus. Strengthen your prayer — it will protect you from what pulls you away.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with sin. The solution starts with prayer — it is a built-in protection system.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الْمُزَّمِّلُ قُمِ اللَّيْلَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا نِّصْفَهُ أَوِ انقُصْ مِنْهُ قَلِيلًا أَوْ زِدْ عَلَيْهِ وَرَتِّلِ الْقُرْآنَ تَرْتِيلًا
translation: O you who wraps himself, arise to pray at night, except for a little — half of it or slightly less, or add to it, and recite the Quran with measured recitation.
tafsir: Tahajjud is the prayer of the closest servants to Allah. Even a little night worship transforms the soul.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless. The cure is in the last third of the night — even two rak'ahs of tahajjud can change everything.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels spiritually disconnected. The night prayer is the secret weapon of the believers.

arabic: وَعِبَادُ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ هَوْنًا وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ الْجَاهِلُونَ قَالُوا سَلَامًا وَالَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمْ سُجَّدًا وَقِيَامًا
translation: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth humbly, and when the ignorant address them, they say words of peace. And those who spend the night prostrating and standing before their Lord.
tafsir: The servants of Ar-Rahman are defined by humility, patience with ignorance, and night worship — a complete spiritual profile.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness shows you know your struggles. Tonight, try one sajdah in the night — be among the servants of Ar-Rahman.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants to know what a true Muslim looks like. This ayah paints the complete picture.

arabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
translation: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
tafsir: The purpose of existence is worship. Everything else — career, family, wealth — is the means, not the end.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs strengthening. Remember your core purpose: you were created to worship Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone searching for life's purpose. The answer is in the very reason they exist.

arabic: الْمَالُ وَالْبَنُونَ زِينَةُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَالْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ خَيْرٌ عِندَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ أَمَلًا
translation: Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of this world. But the enduring good deeds are better with your Lord for reward and better for hope.
tafsir: Dunya is temporary decoration — the lasting investments are good deeds that carry forward to the akhirah.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. Dunya is beautiful but temporary. Invest in what lasts — good deeds, not just wealth.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone chasing only worldly success. The real investment is in what endures beyond this life.

arabic: وَالْعَصْرِ إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ
translation: By time, indeed mankind is in loss, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds and advise each other to truth and advise each other to patience.
tafsir: Allah swears by time — every moment is either gain or loss. The formula: faith + action + truth + patience = salvation.
reflectionTemplate: Your time barakah is low. Time is either gaining or losing — there is no standing still. Invest your hours wisely.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels they are wasting their life. The formula for success is right here in 3 ayahs.

arabic: وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِي عَنِّي فَإِنِّي قَرِيبٌ ۖ أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الدَّاعِ إِذَا دَعَانِ
translation: And when My servants ask you about Me — indeed I am near. I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me.
tafsir: Allah is near — closer than we think. He responds to every dua, though the answer may come in unexpected forms.
reflectionTemplate: Your duas may feel unanswered, but Allah is near. He responds — sometimes differently than we expect, but always with wisdom.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Allah is far away. He says 'I am near' — the distance is from our side, not His.

arabic: وَقَالَ رَبُّكُمُ ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ
translation: And your Lord says: Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
tafsir: Allah commands us to make dua — and promises to respond. The act of calling upon Him is itself an act of worship.
reflectionTemplate: You have active duas waiting. Keep calling — Allah has commanded you to ask, and He has promised to respond.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who has stopped making dua. Allah Himself is asking them to call upon Him.

arabic: لَئِن شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ
translation: If you are grateful, I will surely increase you.
tafsir: Gratitude is the multiplier of blessings. The more you thank Allah, the more He gives — in rizq, peace, and barakah.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq barakah is strong — Allah has blessed you. Increase in shukr, and He will increase you further.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants more from life. The secret is not working harder — it is being more grateful.

arabic: إِنَّ الْأَبْرَارَ يَشْرَبُونَ مِن كَأْسٍ كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا كَافُورًا عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ اللَّهِ يُفَجِّرُونَهَا تَفْجِيرًا
translation: Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup whose mixture is of Kafur, a spring from which the servants of Allah will drink; they will make it gush forth in abundance.
tafsir: The reward of the righteous in Jannah is beyond imagination — rivers of pure, delicious drink flowing eternally.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs a boost. The reward waiting for you in Jannah is beyond anything this world can offer.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is attached to worldly pleasures. The real enjoyment is in the hereafter.

arabic: مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً
translation: Whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while being a believer — We will surely cause them to live a good life.
tafsir: The promise of a 'good life' (hayatan tayyibah) is tied to faith + action — not wealth or status.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah scores show where life feels restricted. A good life comes from righteous action, not just circumstances.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who equates a good life with wealth. Allah defines the good life differently — through faith and action.

arabic: كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ ۗ وَإِنَّمَا تُوَفَّوْنَ أُجُورَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
translation: Every soul will taste death. And you will only be given your full compensation on the Day of Resurrection.
tafsir: Death is certain — the real account is on Judgment Day. This life is temporary; the akhirah is forever.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. Death is certain — every day is one day closer. Prepare for the real life.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone living as if they will never die. The reminder of death puts everything in perspective.

arabic: اعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَزِينَةٌ وَتَفَاخُرٌ بَيْنَكُمْ وَتَكَاثُرٌ فِي الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَوْلَادِ
translation: Know that the life of this world is but amusement and diversion and adornment and boasting to one another and competition in increase of wealth and children.
tafsir: Allah defines dunya precisely — it is play, decoration, and competition. The real life is the akhirah.
reflectionTemplate: You are competing in dunya — wealth, status, comparison. Remember: this is play. The real competition is for Jannah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone consumed by worldly competition. Allah Himself calls it 'play and diversion.'

arabic: وَأْمُرْ أَهْلَكَ بِالصَّلَاةِ وَاصْطَبِرْ عَلَيْهَا
translation: And enjoin prayer upon your family and be steadfast therein.
tafsir: Salah is a family responsibility — leading by example and encouraging those you love to pray.
reflectionTemplate: Your prayer consistency affects not just you but your family. Be the one who leads them to salah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with a parent struggling to get their family to pray. Leadership starts at home.

arabic: وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِّنَ اللَّيْلِ ۚ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَاتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ
translation: And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds remove bad deeds.
tafsir: The five prayers are distributed throughout the day — each one erases sins and elevates your rank.
reflectionTemplate: Your prayer consistency needs work. Each salah erases the sins before it — do not miss this mercy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels weighed down by sins. The solution is in the five daily prayers.

arabic: إِنَّ الصَّلَاةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَّوْقُوتًا
translation: Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers a decree of specified times.
tafsir: Salah is not optional — it is an obligation with specific times. Praying on time is a sign of true faith.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa pulse is low. Salah at its proper time reconnects you with Allah — do not delay it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who prays whenever they feel like it. Salah has appointed times — respect them.

arabic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي صَلَاتِهِمْ خَاشِعُونَ
translation: Certainly will the believers have succeeded: They who are during their prayer humbly submissive.
tafsir: Success is defined by khushu in prayer — not just performing the movements but having a humble, present heart.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is low — your prayers may be happening but without khushu. Focus on being present in your next salah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks prayer is just physical movements. True success is in the humility of the heart.

arabic: أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ إِلَىٰ غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ ۖ إِنَّ قُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ كَانَ مَشْهُودًا
translation: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night and the Quran of dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.
tafsir: Fajr prayer is witnessed by the angels — it has a special status. Waking for Fajr is a sign of strong iman.
reflectionTemplate: Your fajr consistency matters more than you know. The angels witness your dawn prayer — do not miss this blessed time.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who struggles with Fajr. The dawn prayer is witnessed by angels — it is special.

arabic: وَاسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلَاةِ ۚ وَإِنَّهَا لَكَبِيرَةٌ إِلَّا عَلَى الْخَاشِعِينَ
translation: And seek help through patience and prayer, and indeed, it is difficult except for the humbly submissive.
tafsir: Patience and prayer are the two tools for every difficulty. Prayer is hard only for those without khushu.
reflectionTemplate: You are facing difficulty. The tools are patience and prayer — but only if your heart is humble in salah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone going through hardship. The two solutions Allah gives: patience and prayer.

arabic: وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتُ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ
translation: The believing men and believing women are allies of one another. They enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong.
tafsir: Believers are allies — they support each other in good and protect each other from evil. Brotherhood in deen.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs strengthening. Be an ally to other believers — enjoin good, forbid evil, and support each other.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is a solo journey. Believers are allies — we are stronger together.

arabic: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِخْوَةٌ
translation: The believers are but brothers.
tafsir: Brotherhood in faith is a fundamental bond — stronger than blood ties in many ways. It is built on shared faith.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness is low. Start with your Muslim brothers and sisters — the bond of faith is the strongest bond.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling isolated. The believers are brothers — find your community.

arabic: ادْعُ إِلَىٰ سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ
translation: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction.
tafsir: Dawah is done with wisdom and beautiful preaching — not force, not argument, but gentle invitation.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness needs growth. Start small — invite with wisdom and good character, not lectures.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is afraid of giving dawah. It is not about being a scholar — it is about wisdom and beauty.

arabic: وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ قَوْلًا مِّمَّن دَعَا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا وَقَالَ إِنَّنِي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
translation: And who is better in speech than one who invites to Allah and does righteousness and says: Indeed, I am of the Muslims.
tafsir: The best speech is dawah — inviting to Allah with good deeds and declaring your identity as a Muslim.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah potential is untapped. You do not need to be a scholar — just invite with good deeds and sincerity.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants to make a difference. The best speech is inviting others to Allah.

arabic: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الْخَوْفِ وَالْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَنفُسِ وَالثَّمَرَاتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ أُولَٰئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَاتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُهْتَدُونَ
translation: And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient who, when disaster strikes, say: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return. Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy, and it is those who are the guided.
tafsir: Tests are guaranteed — fear, hunger, loss. But the patient ones who say 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' receive blessings, mercy, and guidance.
reflectionTemplate: You are being tested. The response of the patient is 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' — and with that comes blessings and mercy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in grief. The response to loss is not despair — it is 'We belong to Allah and to Him we return.'

arabic: إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ اللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَإِذَا تُلِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُهُ زَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَانًا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا
translation: The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts become fearful, and when His verses are recited to them, it increases them in faith; and upon their Lord they rely. The ones who establish prayer, and from what We have provided them, they spend. Those are the believers, true believers.
tafsir: True faith is defined by fear of Allah, growing iman, tawakkul, salah, and generosity — a complete spiritual profile.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa pulse shows your God-consciousness. When Allah is mentioned, does your heart tremble? That is the sign of true faith.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wonders what true faith looks like. This ayah gives the complete picture.

arabic: مَا أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُ
translation: No disaster strikes except by the permission of Allah. And whoever believes in Allah — He will guide his heart.
tafsir: Everything happens by Allah's will. Faith in this truth guides the heart through any difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception needs strengthening. Nothing happens randomly — everything is by Allah's permission. Trust His wisdom.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who blames luck or fate. Everything is by Allah's permission — and faith guides the heart.

arabic: وَعِندَهُ مَفَاتِحُ الْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَا إِلَّا هُوَ
translation: And with Him are the keys of the unseen; none knows them except Him.
tafsir: Only Allah knows the unseen — the future, the hidden wisdom behind events. Trusting Him means accepting what we cannot see.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception is off. You are trying to see what only Allah can see. Trust His knowledge — He has the keys to the unseen.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone anxious about the future. Only Allah knows what is coming — trust the One who holds the keys.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عِندَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ وَيُنَزِّلُ الْغَيْثَ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِي الْأَرْحَامِ
translation: Indeed, Allah has knowledge of the Hour and sends down rain and knows what is in the wombs.
tafsir: Allah alone knows the unseen — the Hour, the rain, what is in the womb. Human knowledge is limited; His is infinite.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs a reset. The Hour is coming — only Allah knows when. Prepare as if it is tomorrow.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they can predict or control everything. Only Allah knows the unseen.

arabic: وَلَوْ بَسَطَ اللَّهُ الرِّزْقَ لِعِبَادِهِ لَبَغَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَٰكِن يُنَزِّلُ بِقَدَرٍ مَّا يَشَاءُ
translation: And if Allah had extended provision to His servants, they would have transgressed on earth. But He sends down what He wills in a measured way.
tafsir: Allah gives rizq in measured amounts — not because He cannot give more, but because unrestricted wealth leads to corruption.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq barakah is low. But Allah gives what you need — not always what you want. Trust His distribution.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone complaining about their provision. Allah gives in measure — for your own protection.

arabic: وَجَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ آيَتَيْنِ ۖ فَمَحَوْنَا آيَةَ اللَّيْلِ وَجَعَلْنَا آيَةَ النَّهَارِ مُبْصِرَةً وَكُلَّ إِنسَانٍ أَلْزَمْنَاهُ طَائِرَهُ فِي عُنُقِهِ
translation: And We have made the night and day two signs. Then We removed the sign of the night and made the sign of the day visible, that you may seek bounty from your Lord and know the number of years and account.
tafsir: Day and night are signs from Allah — each has its purpose. The cycle of time reminds us of Allah's wisdom and our accountability.
reflectionTemplate: Your time barakah is low. Day and night are signs — use them wisely. Every hour is an amanah from Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wastes time. Day and night are signs — each hour has a purpose.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ أَنتُمُ الْفُقَرَاءُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ۖ وَاللَّهُ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ
translation: O mankind, you are those in need of Allah, while Allah is the Free of need, the Praiseworthy.
tafsir: Every human is in need of Allah — He is the Self-Sufficient. Our poverty to Him is absolute; His richness is absolute.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah scores show areas of need. Remember: you are in need of Allah in everything. Turn to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they are self-sufficient. Every human is in need of Allah — He is the Free of need.

arabic: وَإِذَا أَذَقْنَا النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً فَرِحُوا بِهَا ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَإِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ كَفُورٌ
translation: And when We let the people taste mercy, they rejoice therein, but if evil afflicts them for what their hands have put forth, immediately they despair.
tafsir: Humans tend to be grateful in ease and despair in difficulty. True faith maintains gratitude and patience in both states.
reflectionTemplate: Your sabr and shukr need balance. Be grateful in ease and patient in difficulty — do not let circumstances define your iman.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose faith depends on circumstances. True faith is consistent in both ease and hardship.

arabic: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَعْلَمَ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ مِنكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ وَنَبْلُوَّ أَخْبَارَكُمْ
translation: And We will surely test you until We make evident those who strive among you and the patient, and We will test your affairs.
tafsir: Tests reveal who truly strives and who is patient. Allah tests to elevate, not to punish.
reflectionTemplate: You are being tested. This test is revealing your true level — strive and be patient. The reward is with Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels tested beyond measure. Tests reveal the mujahid and the patient — be among them.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ ۖ وَإِن تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا
translation: Indeed, Allah does not do injustice, even as much as an atom's weight. And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it.
tafsir: Allah's justice is absolute — not an atom's weight of injustice. And good deeds are multiplied — His generosity exceeds His justice.
reflectionTemplate: Your fahm shows concerns about fairness. Allah does not do injustice — and He multiplies every good deed. Trust His justice.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who questions divine justice. Allah does not do injustice — even an atom's weight of it.

arabic: وَهُوَ اللَّهُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ ۖ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّكُمْ وَجَهْرَكُمْ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُونَ
translation: And He is Allah in the heavens and on earth. He knows your secret and what you declare, and He knows what you earn.
tafsir: Allah knows everything — your secrets, your public actions, your earnings. Nothing is hidden from Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness is low. Allah knows what you hide and what you show. Be honest with yourself — He already knows.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they can hide. Allah knows everything — secrets and public actions alike.

arabic: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنسَانَ وَنَعْلَمُ مَا تُوَسْوِسُ بِهِ نَفْسُهُ ۖ وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ
translation: And We have already created man and know what his soul whispers to him, and We are closer to him than his jugular vein.
tafsir: Allah knows every whisper of your nafs — and He is closer to you than your own blood. The distance is from your side, not His.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness shows you know your struggles. Allah knows every whisper — and He is closer than your jugular vein.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels far from Allah. He is closer than your jugular vein — the distance is from your side.

arabic: أَيَحْسَبُ الْإِنسَانُ أَن يُتْرَكَ سُدًى
translation: Does man think that he will be left neglected?
tafsir: Every human will be questioned and accounted for. No one is left without purpose or accountability.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. You will not be left neglected — you will be questioned about every moment.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who lives without purpose. No human is left neglected — every moment is accountable.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنسَانُ مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ الَّذِي خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّاكَ فَعَدَلَكَ فِي أَيِّ صُورَةٍ مَّا شَاءَ رَكَّبَكَ
translation: O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous, who created you, proportioned you, and balanced you? In whatever form He willed, He assembled you.
tafsir: Allah asks what deceived you about His generosity. He created you perfectly — how can you doubt His care?
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah perception is distorted. What deceived you about your Lord? He created you perfectly — trust His care.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who doubts Allah's care. He created you perfectly — what deceived you about His generosity?

arabic: وَأَن لَّيْسَ لِلْإِنسَانِ إِلَّا مَا سَعَىٰ وَأَنَّ سَعْيَهُ سَوْفَ يُرَىٰ
translation: And that there is nothing for man except what he strives for, and that his effort will be seen.
tafsir: Every effort counts — nothing is wasted. What you strive for in this life will be seen and rewarded.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm attitude shows you know what to do. Now strive — every effort will be seen and rewarded by Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks effort is wasted. Nothing is wasted — every strive will be seen and rewarded.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ارْكَعُوا وَاسْجُدُوا وَاعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمْ وَافْعَلُوا الْخَيْرَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
translation: O you who have believed, bow and prostrate and worship your Lord and do good that you may succeed.
tafsir: Success comes from worship (bowing, prostrating) AND doing good — faith and action together.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm attitude is growing. Combine knowledge with action — bow, prostrate, worship, and do good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who knows but does not act. Knowledge without action is incomplete — bow and do good.

arabic: قُلْ إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ أَنَّمَا إِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ
translation: Say: I am only a man like you, to whom it has been revealed that your god is one God.
tafsir: The Prophet ﷺ was human — the message is for all humans. Islam is accessible to everyone, not just the elite.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness needs growth. The Prophet ﷺ was human like you — your dawah starts with being relatable.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is only for the 'religious.' The Prophet ﷺ was human — the message is for everyone.

arabic: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
translation: And We have not sent you, O Muhammad, except as a mercy to the worlds.
tafsir: The Prophet ﷺ was sent as mercy — not punishment, not force. Islam is mercy to all creation.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah approach needs softening. The Prophet ﷺ was mercy — your dawah should be mercy too.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is harsh. The Prophet ﷺ was sent as mercy to all worlds — not punishment.

arabic: إِنَّمَا أَمْرُهُ إِذَا أَرَادَ شَيْئًا أَن يَقُولَ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
translation: His command is only when He intends a thing that He says to it, 'Be,' and it is.
tafsir: Allah's power is absolute — He creates by saying 'Be.' Nothing is difficult for Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception needs this reminder. Nothing is difficult for Allah — He says 'Be' and it is. Trust His power.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks their situation is impossible. For Allah, nothing is impossible — He says 'Be' and it is.

arabic: الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَدًا وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ شَرِيكٌ فِي الْمُلْكِ
translation: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion.
tafsir: Allah owns everything — alone, without partner. All dominion belongs to Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this truth. Allah owns everything — your wealth, your health, your life. It all belongs to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone attached to worldly possessions. Everything belongs to Allah — we are just temporary custodians.

arabic: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ وَاتَّقَىٰ وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنَىٰ فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرَىٰ
translation: As for he who gives and fears Allah and believes in the best reward, We will ease him toward ease.
tafsir: Generosity + taqwa + belief in good = ease from Allah. The path to ease is through giving and fearing Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah is connected to your generosity and taqwa. Give, fear Allah, and believe in the best — ease will come.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with barakah. The formula: give, fear Allah, believe in the best — ease follows.

arabic: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَامْشُوا فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا مِن رِّزْقِهِ
translation: It is He who made the earth tame for you, so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision.
tafsir: The earth is made for you — walk, explore, eat from Allah's provision. He created it all for your benefit.
reflectionTemplate: Your health barakah is low but the earth is made for you. Walk, move, explore — your body is an amanah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels trapped by health issues. The earth is made for you — walk and eat from His provision.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَتْكُم مَّوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَشِفَاءٌ لِّمَا فِي الصُّدُورِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
translation: O mankind, there has come to you instruction from your Lord and healing for what is in the hearts and guidance and mercy for the believers.
tafsir: The Quran is a healing for hearts — not just a book to read but medicine for the soul. Guidance and mercy for believers.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless. The Quran is the healing — even one ayah can bring peace. Read tonight.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone with a troubled heart. The Quran is the healing — not just for knowledge but for the heart.

arabic: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ مَا هُوَ شِفَاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
translation: And We send down of the Quran that which is healing and mercy for the believers.
tafsir: The Quran itself is mercy and healing — its recitation brings peace, its understanding brings guidance.
reflectionTemplate: You are disconnected from the Quran. It is your healing and mercy — do not deprive yourself of it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling emotionally. The Quran is healing and mercy — start with one ayah tonight.

arabic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّىٰ بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ
translation: He has certainly succeeded who purifies himself and remembers the name of his Lord and prays. But you prefer the worldly life, while the Hereafter is better and more enduring.
tafsir: Success is in purification, remembrance, and prayer. But humans prefer dunya — while akhirah is better and lasting.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. You prefer dunya, but akhirah is better and more lasting. Shift your focus.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone chasing only this life. The hereafter is better and more enduring — do not trade it for dunya.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ
translation: O you who have believed, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice. He will amend for you your deeds.
tafsir: Taqwa + truthful speech = reformed deeds. What you say reflects your God-consciousness.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs work. Fear Allah and speak truthfully — He will fix your deeds through your words.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who struggles with speech. Fear Allah and speak truth — He will fix everything else.

arabic: وَلَا تَهِنُوا وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَنتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
translation: So do not weaken and do not grieve, and you will be superior if you are true believers.
tafsir: Allah commands believers not to weaken or grieve — faith gives you the upper hand. Believers are the superior ones.
reflectionTemplate: You are feeling weak or sad. But Allah says you are superior — if you truly believe. Stand tall in your faith.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling defeated. Believers are the superior ones — do not weaken or grieve.

arabic: لَا تَحْزَنْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَنَا
translation: Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us.
tafsir: Said by the Prophet ﷺ to Abu Bakr in the cave — Allah's presence removes all fear and grief.
reflectionTemplate: You are in a difficult situation. Remember what the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Do not grieve, Allah is with us.' He is with you too.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in crisis. The Prophet ﷺ said it in the cave: 'Allah is with us.' He is with you too.

arabic: وَلَا تَيْأَسُوا مِن رَّوْحِ اللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُ لَا يَيْأَسُ مِن رَّوْحِ اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْكَافِرُونَ
translation: And do not despair of relief from Allah. Indeed, no one despairs of relief from Allah except the disbelieving people.
tafsir: Despairing of Allah's relief is a trait of disbelievers. Believers always have hope — Allah's relief is always coming.
reflectionTemplate: You are losing hope. But despairing of Allah's mercy is not from the believers. Relief is coming — hold on.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who has given up hope. Only disbelievers despair of Allah's relief — you are a believer.

arabic: لِيُنفِقْ ذُو سَعَةٍ مِّن سَعَتِهِ ۖ وَمَن قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُ فَلْيُنفِقْ مِمَّا آتَاهُ اللَّهُ ۖ لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا مَا آتَاهَا
translation: Let a man of wealth spend from his wealth, and he whose provision is restricted — let him spend from what Allah has given him. Allah does not charge a soul except with what He has given it.
tafsir: Give from what you have — not more, not less. Allah only asks you to give from what He Himself gave you.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq feels limited. But Allah only asks you to give from what He gave you — no more. Do what you can.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels they cannot give because they are poor. Give from what Allah gave you — He does not overburden.

arabic: مَّثَلُ الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ كَمَثَلِ حَبَّةٍ أَنبَتَتْ سَبْعَ سَنَابِلَ فِي كُلِّ سُنبُلَةٍ مِّائَةُ حَبَّةٍ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يُضَاعِفُ لِمَن يَشَاءُ
translation: The example of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah is like a seed which grows seven ears; in each ear is a hundred grains. And Allah multiplies for whom He wills.
tafsir: Spending in Allah's way is like a seed that grows 700-fold. Allah multiplies your charity beyond imagination.
reflectionTemplate: Your generosity affects your barakah. Every charity you give grows 700-fold with Allah. Give and watch your barakah increase.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone hesitant to give charity. Your charity grows 700-fold — Allah multiplies what you spend in His way.

arabic: وَابْتَغِ فِيمَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ ۖ وَلَا تَنسَ نَصِيبَكَ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا
translation: And seek through that which Allah has given you the home of the Hereafter; and do not forget your share of the world.
tafsir: Balance between dunya and akhirah — use what Allah gave you for the hereafter, but do not neglect your worldly share.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is off-balance. Seek the hereafter with what Allah gave you — but do not forget your worldly share either.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam means abandoning the world. Balance: seek the hereafter, but do not forget dunya.

arabic: مَّا أَصَابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ فَمِنَ اللَّهِ ۖ وَمَا أَصَابَكَ مِن سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِن نَّفْسِكَ
translation: What comes to you of good is from Allah, but what comes to you of evil is from yourself.
tafsir: Good comes from Allah; evil comes from our own actions. Be grateful for blessings and take responsibility for mistakes.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness needs this truth. Good is from Allah — be grateful. Bad is from yourself — take responsibility and improve.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who blames Allah for bad things. Good is from Allah; bad is from ourselves — take responsibility.

arabic: قُلْ يَا عِبَادِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا فِي هَٰذِهِ الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ
translation: Say: O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good.
tafsir: Allah addresses believing servants — fear Him and do good. Good in this world leads to good in the hereafter.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is the foundation. Fear Allah and do good — the reward is good in this life and the next.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking a good life. The formula: fear Allah and do good — good will come to you.

arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ
translation: Indeed, those who have said, 'Our Lord is Allah' and then remained on a right course — the angels will descend upon them, saying, 'Do not fear and do not grieve but receive good tidings of Paradise, which you were promised.'
tafsir: Those who say 'Our Lord is Allah' and remain steadfast — angels come to them with glad tidings: no fear, no grief, Paradise is yours.
reflectionTemplate: You said 'Rabbuna Allah' — now remain steadfast. The angels will come to you: do not fear, do not grieve. Paradise awaits.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is struggling to stay steadfast. Say 'Rabbuna Allah' and hold firm — Paradise is promised to you.

arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
translation: Indeed, those who have said, 'Our Lord is Allah' and then remained on a right course — there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
tafsir: Steadfastness after declaring faith brings ultimate peace — no fear, no grief. The promise is for those who remain firm.
reflectionTemplate: Your strong streak shows your steadfastness. Continue on this path — no fear, no grief awaits those who remain firm.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is steadfast. The reward: no fear, no grief. Continue on this path — the promise is true.

arabic: أُولَٰئِكَ كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَأَيَّدَهُم بِرُوحٍ مِّنْهُ
translation: Those are the ones in whose hearts Allah has inscribed faith and supported them with a spirit from Him.
tafsir: Allah Himself writes faith in the hearts of true believers and supports them with divine help. Your faith is from Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your faith is a gift from Allah — He wrote it in your heart. Protect it, nurture it, and He will support you further.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who doubts their faith. If you believe, Allah Himself wrote that faith in your heart — He will support you.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ تِجَارَةٍ تُنجِيكُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُجَاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَيُدْخِلْكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ
translation: O you who have believed, shall I guide you to a transaction that will save you from a painful punishment? Believe in Allah and His Messenger and strive in the cause of Allah with your wealth and your lives. That is better for you, if you only knew. He will forgive you your sins and admit you to gardens beneath which rivers flow.
tafsir: Faith + jihad in Allah's way = forgiveness + Paradise. Allah calls it a 'transaction' — the best deal you will ever make.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah and social deen need this motivation. Faith + striving = forgiveness + Paradise. This is the best transaction.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone looking for the best deal in life. Faith + striving = forgiveness + Paradise — the ultimate transaction.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ تَوْبَةً نَّصُوحًا عَسَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ أَن يُكَفِّرَ عَنكُمْ سَيِّئَاتِكُمْ وَيُدْخِلَكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ
translation: O you who have believed, repent to Allah with sincere repentance. Perhaps your Lord will remove from you your misdeeds and admit you to gardens beneath which rivers flow.
tafsir: Sincere repentance (tawbah nasuh) leads to forgiveness and Paradise. It is never too late to turn back to Allah.
reflectionTemplate: You have fallen back. But Allah commands sincere repentance — and He may forgive everything and admit you to Paradise. Turn back now.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they cannot be forgiven. Sincere repentance wipes away sins — it is never too late.

arabic: وَإِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلًا صَالِحًا فَأُولَٰئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ اللَّهُ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ حَسَنَاتٍ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
translation: Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work — for them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful.
tafsir: Allah replaces evil deeds with good deeds for those who repent and believe. Your past sins can become good deeds.
reflectionTemplate: Your past mistakes can become good deeds. Repent, believe, do good — Allah will transform your sins into rewards.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone ashamed of their past. Allah replaces evil with good for those who repent — your sins can become rewards.

arabic: قُلْ إِن كُنتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ اللَّهَ فَاتَّبِعُونِي يُحْبِبْكُمُ اللَّهُ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ
translation: Say: If you should love Allah, then follow me, so Allah will love you and forgive you your sins.
tafsir: Love of Allah is demonstrated by following the Prophet ﷺ. Follow the Sunnah, and Allah will love you and forgive you.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness is low. Show your love for Allah by following the Prophet ﷺ — and Allah will love you back.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who says they love Allah. The proof of love: follow the Prophet ﷺ — and Allah will love you.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَن يَرْتَدَّ مِنكُمْ عَن دِينِهِ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ يُجَاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَلَا يَخَافُونَ لَوْمَةَ لَائِمٍ
translation: O you who have believed, whoever of you should revert from his religion — Allah will bring a people He will love and who will love Him, who are humble toward the believers, powerful against the disbelievers; they strive in the cause of Allah and do not fear the blame of a critic.
tafsir: Allah promises to bring people who love Him and He loves them — humble to believers, strong against disbelievers, fearless in deen.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah courage needs this reminder. Be humble to believers, strong in your deen, and do not fear anyone's criticism.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone afraid to practice their faith openly. Be humble to believers, strong in deen — do not fear criticism.

arabic: وَجَاهِدُوا فِي اللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ ۚ هُوَ اجْتَبَاكُمْ وَمَا جَعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ مِنْ حَرَجٍ
translation: And strive for Allah with the striving due to Him. He has chosen you and has not placed upon you in the religion any difficulty.
tafsir: Jihad in Allah's way — He chose you and made religion easy. There is no hardship in Islam; strive with what you can.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness needs this truth. Allah chose you and made Islam easy — strive in His way with what you can.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who finds Islam difficult. Allah made it easy — strive with what you can, there is no hardship.

arabic: وَالَّذِينَ جَاهَدُوا فِينَا لَنَهْدِيَنَّهُمْ سُبُلَنَا ۚ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَمَعَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
translation: And those who strive for Us — We will surely guide them to Our ways. And indeed, Allah is with the doers of good.
tafsir: Those who strive in Allah's way get guided to His paths. Allah is with those who do good — strive and He will guide you.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm and dawah need this promise. Strive in Allah's way — He will guide you to His paths. He is with the doers of good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking guidance. Strive in Allah's way — He will guide you. He is with those who do good.

arabic: وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دِينًا مِّمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ
translation: And who is better in religion than one who submits himself to Allah while being a doer of good?
tafsir: The best religion: submission to Allah + doing good. Islam is not just belief — it is action with sincerity.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah and social deen need this standard. Submit to Allah and do good — that is the best religion.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone asking what the best religion looks like. Submit to Allah and do good — that is it.

arabic: وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ حُنَفَاءَ وَيُقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَيُؤْتُوا الزَّكَاةَ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ دِينُ الْقَيِّمَةِ
translation: And they were not commanded except to worship Allah, being sincere to Him in religion, inclining to truth, and to establish prayer and give zakah. And that is the correct religion.
tafsir: The correct religion: sincere worship + prayer + zakah. Simplicity itself — worship Allah sincerely and fulfill your duties.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa needs this clarity. Sincere worship + prayer + zakah = the correct religion. Focus on these basics.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone overwhelmed by Islam. The basics: worship Allah sincerely, pray, give zakah — that is the correct religion.

arabic: إِذَا جَاءَ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ وَالْفَتْحُ وَرَأَيْتَ النَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًا فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابًا
translation: When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest, and you see the people entering into the religion of Allah in multitudes, then exalt Him with praise of your Lord and ask forgiveness of Him. Indeed, He is ever Accepting of repentance.
tafsir: When victory comes, the response is praise and seeking forgiveness — not arrogance. Humility in success is the mark of true faith.
reflectionTemplate: Your strong streak is a victory. When Allah gives you success in deen, respond with praise and forgiveness — stay humble.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone experiencing spiritual success. The response to victory: praise Allah and seek forgiveness — stay humble.

arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً كَشَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ أَصْلُهَا ثَابِتٌ وَفَرْعُهَا فِي السَّمَاءِ
translation: Have you not considered how Allah presents an example? A good word is like a good tree, whose root is firmly fixed and its branches in the sky.
tafsir: A good word (faith, dhikr, dawah) is like a tree — firmly rooted, branches in heaven, always producing fruit.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah and dhikr are like a tree — if rooted in faith, they will grow and produce fruit. Keep planting good words.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants to make a lasting impact. A good word is like a tree — rooted firmly, branches in heaven.

arabic: مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ الْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعًا
translation: Whoever desires honor — then to Allah belongs all honor.
tafsir: True honor comes from Allah alone — not from wealth, status, or people. Seek honor through Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs this truth. True honor is from Allah — not from people. Seek honor through faith, not approval.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking approval from people. True honor is from Allah — seek it through faith, not worldly status.

arabic: أَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكَانُوا يَتَّقُونَ لَهُمُ الْبُشْرَىٰ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ
translation: Unquestionably, for the allies of Allah there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve. Those who believed and were fearing Allah — for them is good news in this worldly life and in the Hereafter.
tafsir: Allah's awliya (close friends) have no fear or grief — they get good news in both worlds. The path: faith + taqwa.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa makes you Allah's ally. No fear, no grief — good news in this life and the next. Keep your taqwa strong.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who fears the future. Allah's allies have no fear — faith + taqwa = good news in both worlds.

arabic: مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلِنَفْسِهِ ۖ وَمَنْ أَسَاءَ فَعَلَيْهَا
translation: Whoever does a good deed — it is for himself; and whoever does evil — it is against himself.
tafsir: Every action returns to you — good for you, bad against you. Your choices shape your destiny.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs awareness needs this reminder. Every good deed benefits you; every bad deed harms you. Choose wisely.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone making bad choices. Every action returns to you — good benefits you, bad harms you. Choose wisely.

arabic: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا بِمَا عَمِلُوا وَيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا بِالْحُسْنَى
translation: And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth — that He may recompense those who do evil with what they have done and recompense those who who do good with the best.
tafsir: Allah owns everything — and He will reward perfectly. Evil gets its due; good gets the best reward.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this truth. Allah will reward perfectly — evil gets its due, good gets the best. Keep doing good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone worried about justice. Allah will reward perfectly — evil gets its due, good gets the best reward.

arabic: إِن تُقْرِضُوا اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا يُضَاعِفْهُ لَكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ
translation: If you loan Allah a goodly loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you.
tafsir: Spending in Allah's way is a loan to Allah — He multiplies it and forgives your sins. The best investment.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq barakah is connected to your generosity. Loan Allah a goodly loan — He will multiply it and forgive you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone hesitant to give charity. It is a loan to Allah — He multiplies it and forgives your sins.

arabic: فَقُلْتُ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ إِنَّهُ كَانَ غَفَّارًا يُرْسِلِ السَّمَاءَ عَلَيْكُم مِّدْرَارًا وَيُمْدِدْكُم بِأَمْوَالٍ وَبَنِينَ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ أَنْهَارًا
translation: And said, 'Ask forgiveness of your Lord. Indeed, He is ever a Perpetual Forgiver. He will send rain from the sky upon you in continuing showers and give you increase in wealth and children and provide for you gardens and provide for you rivers.'
tafsir: Istighfar (seeking forgiveness) brings rain, wealth, children, gardens, and rivers. The formula for barakah: repent to Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah scores need this formula. Istighfar brings rain, wealth, children, gardens. Repent and watch your barakah increase.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with barakah. The formula: seek forgiveness — rain, wealth, children, gardens will follow.

arabic: يَا أَيَّتُهَا النَّفْسُ الْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ ارْجِعِي إِلَىٰ رَبِّكِ رَاضِيَةً مَّرْضِيَّةً فَادْخُلِي فِي عِبَادِي وَادْخُلِي جَنَّتِي
translation: O reassured soul, return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing to Him, and enter among My servants, and enter My Paradise.
tafsir: The ultimate goal: a soul at peace, returning to Allah pleased and pleasing Him, entering Paradise among His servants.
reflectionTemplate: Your spiritual journey leads here — a soul at peace, returning to Allah pleased. Strive for this ending: enter His Paradise.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking peace. The goal: a soul at peace, returning to Allah, entering Paradise. This is the ultimate success.

arabic: نَبِّئْ عِبَادِي أَنِّي أَنَا الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
translation: Inform My servants that I am the Forgiving, the Merciful.
tafsir: Allah commands the Prophet ﷺ to announce His forgiveness and mercy. This is the message: Allah forgives and has mercy.
reflectionTemplate: You need to hear this from Allah Himself: He is Al-Ghafoor, Ar-Raheem. He forgives and has mercy — turn to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels unforgiven. Allah Himself says: I am the Forgiving, the Merciful. Turn to Him.

arabic: لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
translation: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been among the wrongdoers.
tafsir: The dua of Yunus ﷺ in the belly of the whale — the greatest dua of repentance. Whoever says it, Allah answers.
reflectionTemplate: You are in difficulty from your own sins. Say the dua of Yunus: 'La ilaha illa anta, subhanaka, inni kuntu min ad-dhalimin.' Allah will answer.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in crisis from their own mistakes. The dua of Yunus in the whale — say it and Allah will answer.

arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ
translation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve.
tafsir: The dua of Sulayman ﷺ — asking for ability to be grateful and do good. Gratitude is a gift from Allah Himself.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah is a blessing from Allah. Ask Him to enable you to be grateful — gratitude itself is a gift from Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone blessed with wealth or health. The dua of Sulayman: ask Allah to enable you to be grateful.

arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَاجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّاتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ وَاجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
translation: Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us leaders for the righteous.
tafsir: The dua of the believers: family as comfort + leadership among the righteous. The best dua for family and influence.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen and family need this dua. Ask Allah: make my family comfort for my eyes and make me a leader among the righteous.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking a righteous family. The dua: make my family comfort for my eyes and make me a leader among the righteous.

arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي وَاحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِي يَفْقَهُوا قَوْلِي
translation: My Lord, expand for me my breast and ease for me my task and untie the knot from my tongue that they may understand my speech.
tafsir: The dua of Musa ﷺ before confronting Pharaoh — for confidence, ease, and clear speech. The ultimate dua for dawah courage.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness needs this dua. Ask Allah: expand my chest, ease my task, loosen my tongue. He will give you courage.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone afraid to speak about Islam. The dua of Musa before Pharaoh — ask for confidence and clear speech.

arabic: قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ مَالِكَ الْمُلْكِ تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ مَن تَشَاءُ وَتَنزِعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّن تَشَاءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَن تَشَاءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَن تَشَاءُ ۖ بِيَدِكَ الْخَيْرُ
translation: Say: O Allah, Owner of Sovereignty, You give sovereignty to whom You will and You take sovereignty away from whom You will. You honor whom You will and You humble whom You will. In Your hand is all good.
tafsir: Allah controls all power, honor, and humiliation. All good is in His hand — seek honor and power from Him alone.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception and dawah courage need this. All power and honor are in Allah's hand — seek from Him alone.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking power or honor. All good is in Allah's hand — seek from Him, not from people.

arabic: فَمَن تَبِعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
translation: And whoever follows My guidance — there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve. But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs — those are companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.
tafsir: Following Allah's guidance = no fear, no grief. Denying His signs = Hellfire. The choice is clear — follow the guidance.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is the key. Follow Allah's guidance — no fear, no grief. The alternative is not worth considering. Stay on the path.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone hesitating about Islam. Follow Allah's guidance: no fear, no grief. The choice is clear.

arabic: وَلَقَدْ بَعَثْنَا فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولًا أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَاجْتَنِبُوا الطَّاغُوتَ
translation: And We certainly sent into every nation a messenger, saying, 'Worship Allah and avoid false gods.'
tafsir: Every nation received the same message: worship Allah alone. This is the universal truth — you are part of a timeless tradition.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah is part of a timeless tradition. Every nation received this message: worship Allah alone. You are continuing this legacy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone new to Islam. This message has been sent to every nation: worship Allah alone. You are part of a timeless tradition.

arabic: إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ مَنِ اتَّبَعَ الذِّكْرَ وَخَشِيَ الرَّحْمَٰنَ بِالْغَيْبِ ۖ فَبَشِّرْهُ بِمَغْفِرَةٍ وَأَجْرٍ كَرِيمٍ
translation: You can only warn one who follows the message and fears the Most Merciful unseen. So give him good tidings of forgiveness and noble reward.
tafsir: Dawah is for those who follow the reminder and fear Allah unseen. Give them glad tidings: forgiveness and a noble reward.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah target: those who already follow the reminder and fear Allah. Give them glad tidings: forgiveness and noble reward.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone already inclined to faith. Give them glad tidings: forgiveness and a noble reward await them.

arabic: الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلَامَ دِينًا
translation: This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion.
tafsir: Allah perfected Islam — it is complete, approved, and the final religion. You have the complete guidance — follow it with confidence.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah confidence needs this truth. Allah perfected Islam — it is complete and approved. Follow it and share it with confidence.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone doubting Islam. Allah Himself perfected it and approved it — it is complete. Follow with confidence.

arabic: إِنَّا فَتَحْنَا لَكَ فَتْحًا مُّبِينًا
translation: Indeed, We have given you a clear conquest.
tafsir: Allah gave the Prophet ﷺ a clear victory — after years of struggle. Victory comes after patience and steadfastness.
reflectionTemplate: Your strong streak will lead to clear victory. After years of struggle, Allah gave the Prophet ﷺ a clear conquest. Your victory is coming.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling in their faith. After struggle comes clear victory — Allah promised it. Hold on.

arabic: أَلَمْ نَشْرَحْ لَكَ صَدْرَكَ وَوَضَعْنَا عَنكَ وِزْرَكَ الَّذِي أَنقَضَ ظَهْرَكَ وَرَفَعْنَا لَكَ ذِكْرَكَ فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا فَإِذَا فَرَغْتَ فَانصَبْ وَإِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَارْغَب
translation: Did We not expand for you your breast? And We removed from you your burden which had weighed upon your back? And raised high for you your repute? For indeed, with hardship will be ease. Indeed, with hardship will be ease. So when you have finished, then stand up for supplication. And to your Lord direct your longing.
tafsir: Allah expanded the Prophet's chest, removed his burden, raised his name — then promised ease with hardship twice. After struggle: stand and pray to Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty is temporary. Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. When you finish this hardship, stand and turn to your Lord.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in deep struggle. Allah expanded the Prophet's chest after difficulty — He will expand yours too. Ease is coming.

arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
translation: Say: He is Allah, the One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born. Nor is there to Him any equivalent.
tafsir: Surah Ikhlas — the essence of tawheed. Allah is One, the Eternal Refuge, without equal. Reciting it is like reciting one-third of the Quran.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa needs this foundation. Allah is One, the Eternal Refuge — turn to Him alone. He has no equal, no partner.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking the essence of Islam. Allah is One, the Eternal Refuge — this is the foundation of everything.

arabic: قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا الْكَافِرُونَ لَا أَعْبُدُ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ وَلَا أَنتُمْ عَابِدُونَ مَا أَعْبُدُ وَلَا أَنَا عَابِدٌ مَّا عَبَدتُّمْ وَلَا أَنتُمْ عَابِدُونَ مَا أَعْبُدُ لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ وَلِيَ دِينِ
translation: Say: O disbelievers, I do not worship what you worship. Nor are you worshippers of what I worship. Nor will I be a worshipper of what you worship. Nor will you be worshippers of what I worship. For you is your religion, and for me is my religion.
tafsir: The Surah of Disavowal — clear separation from false worship. Your identity is clear: you worship Allah alone, and that is your path.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah courage needs this clarity. Your path is clear: you worship Allah alone. Do not be ashamed — declare it openly.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone afraid to declare their faith. Your identity is clear: you worship Allah alone. Declare it openly.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلَاةِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ
translation: O you who have believed, seek help through patience and prayer. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
tafsir: Allah is with those who are patient — He promises His companionship to those who endure. Patience and prayer are the tools.
reflectionTemplate: You are struggling. Seek help through patience and prayer — Allah is with the patient. He is with you right now.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in difficulty. The solution: patience and prayer. And the promise: Allah is with you.

arabic: وَكَأَيِّن مِّن نَّبِيٍّ قَاتَلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ فَمَا وَهَنُوا لِمَا أَصَابَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ
translation: And how many a prophet fought and with him fought many religious scholars. But they never lost assurance due to what afflicted them in the cause of Allah.
tafsir: Prophets and their followers never weakened despite hardship. They did not give up — and neither should you.
reflectionTemplate: You are feeling weak from your struggles. But the prophets and their followers never weakened — do not give up either.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who wants to give up. The prophets fought with thousands — they never weakened. Do not give up.

arabic: الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ فِي السَّرَّاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ وَالْكَاظِمِينَ الْغَيْظَ وَالْعَافِينَ عَنِ النَّاسِ
translation: Who spend during ease and hardship and who restrain anger and who pardon the people.
tafsir: The qualities of the righteous: give in ease and hardship, control anger, forgive people. Allah loves those who do good.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs these qualities: give in all circumstances, control your anger, forgive others. Allah loves the doers of good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with anger or forgiveness. The righteous: give always, control anger, forgive people.

arabic: الَّذِينَ قَالَ لَهُمُ النَّاسُ إِنَّ النَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا لَكُمْ فَاخْشَوْهُمْ فَزَادَهُمْ إِيمَانًا وَقَالُوا حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
translation: Those to whom people said: Indeed, the people have gathered against you, so fear them. But it increased them in faith, and they said: Sufficient for us is Allah, and He is the best Disposer of affairs.
tafsir: When told enemies gathered against them, their faith INCREASED. They said 'Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal wakeel' — and Allah proved sufficient.
reflectionTemplate: You face opposition. But like the believers, let it increase your faith. Say: Allah is sufficient for me and the best disposer of affairs.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone facing opposition. The response: increase in faith and say 'Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal wakeel.'

arabic: وَاعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَلَا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئًا وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا وَبِذِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَالْيَتَامَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينِ
translation: And worship Allah and do not associate anything with Him and to parents do good and to relatives, orphans, the needy.
tafsir: Worship Allah alone + be good to parents, relatives, orphans, and the needy. Islam is between you and Allah AND between you and people.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs this balance. Worship Allah alone AND be good to parents, relatives, orphans, and the needy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is only rituals. Worship Allah AND be good to parents, orphans, and the needy.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ ۖ وَإِن تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا وَيُؤْتِ مِن لَّدُنْهُ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
translation: Indeed, Allah does not do injustice, even as much as an atom's weight. And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it and gives from Himself a great reward.
tafsir: Allah's justice is perfect — not an atom's weight of injustice. And good deeds are multiplied — His generosity exceeds His justice.
reflectionTemplate: You question fairness. Allah does not do injustice — even an atom's weight. And He multiplies good deeds. Trust His justice.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone questioning divine justice. Allah does not do injustice — and He multiplies every good deed.

arabic: مَّا أَصَابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ فَمِنَ اللَّهِ ۖ وَمَا أَصَابَكَ مِن سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِن نَّفْسِكَ
translation: What comes to you of good is from Allah, but what comes to you of evil is from yourself.
tafsir: Good comes from Allah; evil comes from our own actions. Be grateful for blessings and take responsibility for mistakes.
reflectionTemplate: Good is from Allah — be grateful. Bad is from yourself — take responsibility and improve. This is the balance.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone blaming Allah for bad things. Good is from Allah; bad is from ourselves — take responsibility.

arabic: وَلَا تَهِنُوا فِي ابْتِغَاءِ الْقَوْمِ ۖ إِن تَكُونُوا تَأْلَمُونَ فَإِنَّهُمْ يَأْلَمُونَ كَمَا تَأْلَمُونَ
translation: And do not weaken in pursuit of the enemy. If you are suffering, they are suffering as you are suffering.
tafsir: Do not weaken in pursuing what is right. Your enemies suffer too — you are not alone in your struggle.
reflectionTemplate: You are struggling. But do not weaken — your challenges are not unique. Others face difficulty too. Keep going.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling alone in struggle. Do not weaken — others face difficulty too. Keep pursuing what is right.

arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ وَبَعَثْنَا مِنْهُمُ اثْنَيْ عَشَرَ نَقِيبًا
translation: And Allah had already taken the covenant of the Children of Israel, and We delegated from among them twelve leaders.
tafsir: Allah took a covenant from the Children of Israel and appointed leaders. Leadership is a responsibility and a trust from Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen involves leadership. Be a leader in good — it is a trust from Allah. Lead by example.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in a leadership position. Leadership is a trust from Allah — lead by example and with integrity.

arabic: وَمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۖ وَلَلدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ
translation: And the worldly life is not but amusement and diversion; but the home of the Hereafter is best for those who fear Allah.
tafsir: Dunya is play and amusement — the akhirah is the real home for those who have taqwa. Do not be deceived by this world.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. Dunya is play — the real home is the akhirah for those who fear Allah. Shift your focus.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone attached to this world. Dunya is play — the real home is the hereafter for those who fear Allah.

arabic: مَن جَاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثَالِهَا ۖ وَمَن جَاءَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ فَلَا يُجْزَىٰ إِلَّا مِثْلَهَا
translation: Whoever comes with a good deed will have ten times the like thereof, and whoever comes with an evil deed will only be recompensed the like thereof.
tafsir: Good deeds are multiplied tenfold — evil is recompensed equally. Allah's generosity far exceeds His justice.
reflectionTemplate: Every good deed you do is multiplied ten times. Keep doing good — the reward is immense. Evil is only equal — but good is tenfold.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone wondering if good deeds matter. Good is multiplied ten times — evil is only equal. Keep doing good.

arabic: وَلَا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلَاحِهَا وَادْعُوهُ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا
translation: And do not cause corruption on the earth after it has been set in order. And invoke Him in fear and aspiration.
tafsir: Do not corrupt the earth — and call upon Allah with both fear and hope. Dua is the weapon of the believer.
reflectionTemplate: Your duas need this balance. Call upon Allah with both fear and hope — not just when you need something, but always.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who only prays in crisis. Call upon Allah with fear and hope — always, not just in difficulty.

arabic: وَلِلَّهِ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَىٰ فَادْعُوهُ بِهَا
translation: And to Allah belong the best names, so invoke Him by them.
tafsir: Allah has the most beautiful names — call upon Him using them. Each name reveals an attribute you can connect with.
reflectionTemplate: Your dua life needs depth. Call upon Allah by His beautiful names — Ar-Rahman, Ar-Raheem, Al-Ghafoor. Each name is a door.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone learning to make dua. Call upon Allah by His beautiful names — each name opens a different door.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً فَاثْبُتُوا وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
translation: O you who have believed, when you encounter a company, stand firm and remember Allah much that you may be successful.
tafsir: When facing challenges: stand firm and remember Allah. Dhikr in difficulty leads to success.
reflectionTemplate: You are facing a challenge. Stand firm and remember Allah — much dhikr leads to success. Do not waver.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone facing a challenge. The solution: stand firm and remember Allah much. Success comes through dhikr.

arabic: وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ اللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ فَلَا كَاشِفَ لَهُ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَإِن يُرِدْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَلَا رَادَّ لِفَضْلِهِ
translation: And if Allah should touch you with adversity, there is no remover of it except Him. And if He intends for you good, then there is no repeller of His bounty.
tafsir: Only Allah can remove harm or give good. No one can repel His bounty or remove His decree. Turn to Him alone.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty can only be removed by Allah. And His bounty cannot be repelled. Turn to Him — He alone controls all things.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking help from others. Only Allah removes harm and gives good — turn to Him alone.

arabic: وَأَنِ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيْهِ يُمَتِّعْكُم مَّتَاعًا حَسَنًا
translation: And ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. He will give you a good provision.
tafsir: Istighfar + tawbah = good provision from Allah. The formula for barakah: seek forgiveness and repent.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah needs this formula. Seek forgiveness and repent — Allah will give you good provision. Start today.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with provision. The formula: seek forgiveness and repent — Allah will give good provision.

arabic: تِلْكَ مِنْ أَنبَاءِ الْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهَا إِلَيْكَ
translation: That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you.
tafsir: The stories of the prophets are revealed by Allah — they are not fiction. Learn from them and be guided.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm needs the stories of the prophets. They are revealed by Allah — learn from them and be guided.
dawahTemplate: Share the stories of the prophets with others. They are from Allah — full of lessons and guidance.

arabic: قَالَ أَنَا يُوسُفُ وَهَٰذَا أَخِي ۖ قَدْ مَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا
translation: He said: I am Joseph, and this is my brother. Allah has certainly favored us.
tafsir: After years of suffering, Yusuf ﷺ revealed himself and attributed everything to Allah's favor. Patience leads to reunion and success.
reflectionTemplate: Your patience will pay off. After years of suffering, Yusuf said 'Allah has favored us.' Your reunion with ease is coming.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in prolonged hardship. Yusuf ﷺ suffered for years — then said 'Allah has favored us.' Your ease is coming.

arabic: لَّهُ مُعَقِّبَاتٌ مِّن بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ يَحْفَظُونَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ
translation: For each one are successive angels before and behind him who protect him by the decree of Allah.
tafsir: Angels protect you by Allah's command — you are never alone. Guardian angels are with you at all times.
reflectionTemplate: You feel alone and unprotected. But angels surround you by Allah's command — you are never truly alone.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling alone. Angels protect you by Allah's command — you are surrounded by divine protection.

arabic: وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكُمْ لَئِن شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ ۖ وَلَئِن كَفَرْتُمْ إِنَّ عَذَابِي لَشَدِيدٌ
translation: And when your Lord proclaimed: If you are grateful, I will surely increase you; but if you deny, indeed, My punishment is severe.
tafsir: Allah promises increase for gratitude — and warns of punishment for denial. Gratitude is the multiplier of blessings.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah depends on gratitude. Thank Allah for what you have — and He will increase you. Gratitude is the key.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone wanting more. The secret: gratitude. Thank Allah — and He will increase you.

arabic: نَبِّئْ عِبَادِي أَنِّي أَنَا الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
translation: Inform My servants that I am the Forgiving, the Merciful.
tafsir: Allah commands the Prophet ﷺ to announce His forgiveness and mercy. This is the message: Allah forgives and has mercy.
reflectionTemplate: You need to hear this from Allah Himself: He is Al-Ghafoor, Ar-Raheem. He forgives and has mercy — turn to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels unforgiven. Allah Himself says: I am the Forgiving, the Merciful. Turn to Him.

arabic: وَإِن تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا
translation: And if you should count the favors of Allah, you could not enumerate them.
tafsir: Allah's blessings are countless — you cannot even count them. How can you not be grateful?
