reflectionTemplate: Your gratitude needs this perspective. Allah's blessings are countless — you cannot even count them. Be grateful.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone taking blessings for granted. Allah's blessings are countless — you cannot even count them. Be grateful.

arabic: جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ ۖ لَهُمْ فِيهَا مَا يَشَاءُونَ
translation: Gardens of perpetual residence, which they will enter, beneath which rivers flow. They will have therein whatever they wish.
tafsir: In Jannah: perpetual gardens, rivers beneath, and whatever you wish. The reward is beyond imagination.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this vision. In Jannah: gardens, rivers, whatever you wish. Keep striving for it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone needing motivation. Jannah: gardens, rivers, whatever you wish. Keep striving for it.

arabic: وَمَا بِكُم مِّن نِّعْمَةٍ فَمِنَ اللَّهِ
translation: And whatever you have of favor — it is from Allah.
tafsir: Every favor, every blessing, every good thing — it is all from Allah. Nothing comes from your own power.
reflectionTemplate: Your blessings are from Allah — not from your own power. Be grateful and attribute everything to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone proud of their success. Every favor is from Allah — not from your own power. Be grateful.

arabic: وَمَنْ أَرَادَ الْآخِرَةَ وَسَعَىٰ لَهَا سَعْيَهَا وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولَٰئِكَ كَانَ سَعْيُهُم مَّشْكُورًا
translation: And whoever desires the Hereafter and exerts the effort due to it while being a believer — those are the ones whose effort is appreciated.
tafsir: Effort for the akhirah by a believer is appreciated by Allah. Your striving is never wasted — Allah sees and appreciates it.
reflectionTemplate: Your effort for the akhirah is appreciated by Allah. Keep striving — He sees everything and appreciates your effort.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose effort feels wasted. Effort for the akhirah is appreciated by Allah — keep striving.

arabic: وَقُل رَّبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
translation: And say: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up when I was small.
tafsir: The dua for parents — asking Allah to have mercy on them as they raised you. One of the most important duas.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen includes your parents. Make this dua: My Lord, have mercy on them as they raised me when I was small.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose parents are alive or deceased. The dua: My Lord, have mercy on them as they raised me.

arabic: وَاصْبِرْ نَفْسَكَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُم بِالْغَدَاةِ وَالْعَشِيِّ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُ
translation: And keep yourself patient with those who call upon their Lord in the morning and the evening, seeking His countenance.
tafsir: Keep company with those who remember Allah morning and evening. Your companions shape your spiritual state.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs good company. Stay with those who remember Allah morning and evening — they will lift you up.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking good friends. Keep company with those who remember Allah morning and evening.

arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ الْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلًا
translation: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds — they will have the Gardens of Paradise as a lodging.
tafsir: Faith + righteous deeds = Gardens of Paradise. The ultimate reward for the believers who do good.
reflectionTemplate: Your path leads to Paradise — faith + righteous deeds. Keep believing and doing good. Jannatul Firdaws awaits.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking motivation. Faith + righteous deeds = Gardens of Paradise. The ultimate reward.

arabic: قَالَ لَا تَخَافَا ۖ إِنَّنِي مَعَكُمَا أَسْمَعُ وَأَرَىٰ
translation: He said: Fear not. Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see.
tafsir: Allah told Musa and Harun: fear not, I am with you, I hear and see. When Allah is with you, there is nothing to fear.
reflectionTemplate: You are afraid. But Allah says: fear not, I am with you, I hear and see. He is with you right now.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in fear. Allah says: fear not, I am with you, I hear and see. He is with you now.

arabic: قُلْنَا يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
translation: We said: O fire, be coolness and peace upon Abraham.
tafsir: Allah commanded the fire to be coolness and peace for Ibrahim ﷺ. When Allah protects you, even fire becomes cool.
reflectionTemplate: You are in a fiery situation. But Allah can make it coolness and peace — just as He did for Ibrahim. Trust Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in extreme difficulty. Allah made fire coolness for Ibrahim — He can make your difficulty easy.

arabic: وَأَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ الضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
translation: And Job, when he called out to his Lord: Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful.
tafsir: Ayyub ﷺ called upon Allah in his illness: adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful. Allah healed him.
reflectionTemplate: You are suffering. Call upon Allah like Ayyub: adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful. He will heal you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in illness or suffering. Ayyub's dua: adversity has touched me, You are the Most Merciful. Allah healed him.

arabic: وَذَا النُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَاضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِي الظُّلُمَاتِ أَن لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
translation: And the man of the fish, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree anything upon him. And he called out within the darknesses: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been among the wrongdoers.
tafsir: Yunus ﷺ left in anger, thinking Allah would not constrain him. In the belly of the whale, in darkness, he called out. Allah saved him.
reflectionTemplate: You may have made mistakes. But even in the deepest darkness, Yunus's dua worked. Say it now: La ilaha illa anta, subhanaka, inni kuntu min ad-dhalimin.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in deep trouble. Yunus's dua from the belly of the whale: La ilaha illa anta, subhanaka, inni kuntu min ad-dhalimin.

arabic: وَقُل رَّبِّ اغْفِرْ وَارْحَمْ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
translation: And say: My Lord, forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of the merciful.
tafsir: A simple, powerful dua: My Lord, forgive and have mercy. And You are the best of the merciful. Always make this dua.
reflectionTemplate: Your dua life needs this simplicity. Say: My Lord, forgive and have mercy, You are the best of the merciful. Make it daily.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone learning to make dua. Simple and powerful: My Lord, forgive and have mercy, You are the best.

arabic: وَلَا يَأْتَلِ أُولُو الْفَضْلِ مِنكُمْ وَالسَّعَةِ أَن يُؤْتُوا أُولِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينَ وَالْمُهَاجِرِينَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ
translation: And let not those of virtue among you and wealth swear not to give to relatives and the needy and the emigrants for the cause of Allah.
tafsir: The wealthy and capable should give to relatives, needy, and those striving for Allah. Generosity is a sign of true faith.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq is a trust from Allah. Give to relatives, the needy, and those striving for Allah. Generosity increases barakah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone blessed with wealth. Give to relatives, the needy, and those in Allah's way — generosity increases barakah.

arabic: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى الْحَيِّ الَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِهِ
translation: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt His praise.
tafsir: Tawakkul on the Ever-Living who never dies. People die, plans fail, but Allah is eternal. Rely on Him alone.
reflectionTemplate: Your tawakkul needs strengthening. Rely on the Ever-Living who never dies — not on people, not on plans. He is eternal.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone relying on people or plans. Rely on the Ever-Living who never dies — He is eternal and reliable.

arabic: وَإِذَا مَرِضْتُ فَهُوَ يَشْفِينِ وَالَّذِي يُمِيتُنِي ثُمَّ يُحْيِينِ
translation: And when I am ill, it is He who cures me. And who will cause me to die and then bring me to life.
tafsir: Ibrahim ﷺ said: when I am ill, He cures me. Allah is Ash-Shaafi — the Healer. Turn to Him in illness.
reflectionTemplate: Your health struggles need Ash-Shaafi. When Ibrahim was ill, he turned to Allah: He cures me. Turn to the Healer.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is ill. Ibrahim said: when I am ill, He cures me. Turn to Ash-Shaafi — the Healer.

arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَالِدَيَّ
translation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents.
tafsir: The dua of Sulayman ﷺ — asking for ability to be grateful. Gratitude itself is a gift from Allah that we must ask for.
reflectionTemplate: Your gratitude needs divine help. Ask like Sulayman: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone blessed. Gratitude is a gift — ask Allah to enable you to be grateful like Sulayman did.

arabic: فَسَقَىٰ لَهُمَا ثُمَّ تَوَلَّىٰ إِلَى الظِّلِّ فَقَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي لِمَا أَنزَلْتَ إِلَيَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَقِيرٌ
translation: So he watered for them; then he went back to the shade and said: My Lord, indeed I am, for whatever good You would send down to me, in need.
tafsir: Musa ﷺ after helping the women, sat in the shade and said: My Lord, I am in need of whatever good You send. Humility before Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah needs this humility. Like Musa, say: My Lord, I am in need of whatever good You send. Humble yourself before Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling self-sufficient. Musa said: I am in need of whatever good You send. Humble yourself before Allah.

arabic: مَن كَانَ يَرْجُو لِقَاءَ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ أَجَلَ اللَّهِ لَآتٍ
translation: Whoever should hope for the meeting with Allah — indeed, the term of Allah is coming.
tafsir: Whoever hopes to meet Allah — the appointed time is coming. Live with this hope and prepare for the meeting.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this hope. Whoever hopes to meet Allah — the appointed time is coming. Prepare for it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone needing purpose. Whoever hopes to meet Allah — the appointed time is coming. Prepare for it.

arabic: وَكَأَيِّن مِّن دَابَّةٍ لَّا تَحْمِلُ رِزْقَهَا اللَّهُ يَرْزُقُهَا وَإِيَّاكُمْ
translation: And how many a creature does not carry its provision. Allah provides for it and for you.
tafsir: Creatures that do not carry their provision — Allah provides for them. He will provide for you too. Trust His provision.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq anxiety needs this reminder. Even creatures that carry no provision — Allah feeds them. He will provide for you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone anxious about provision. Even creatures that carry no food — Allah provides for them. He will provide for you.

arabic: وَإِذَا أَذَقْنَا النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً فَرِحُوا بِهَا
translation: And when We let the people taste mercy, they rejoice therein.
tafsir: Humans rejoice in mercy but despair in hardship. True faith maintains gratitude and patience in both states.
reflectionTemplate: Your faith needs balance. Rejoice in mercy and be patient in hardship — do not let circumstances define your iman.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose faith fluctuates. True faith: rejoice in mercy, patient in hardship. Consistency is key.

arabic: يَا بُنَيَّ أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَانْهَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَاصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا أَصَابَكَ
translation: O my son, establish prayer, enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and be patient over what afflicts you.
tafsir: Luqman's advice to his son: pray, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient. The complete formula for a successful life.
reflectionTemplate: Your life needs this formula from Luqman: pray, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient. Follow this advice.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking life advice. Luqman's wisdom: pray, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient.

arabic: إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْلِمَاتِ وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَالْقَانِتِينَ وَالْقَانِتَاتِ وَالصَّادِقِينَ وَالصَّادِقَاتِ
translation: Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women, the believing men and believing women, the obedient men and obedient women, the truthful men and truthful women.
tafsir: Allah mentions men and women equally in faith, obedience, and truth. Both genders are equal in reward and responsibility.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah includes everyone. Men and women are equal in faith, obedience, and truth — invite all to Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone questioning gender in Islam. Men and women are equal in faith, obedience, and truth before Allah.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ ذِكْرًا كَثِيرًا
translation: O you who have believed, remember Allah with much remembrance.
tafsir: Allah commands much dhikr — not a little, but much. Make dhikr a constant habit throughout your day.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa needs constant dhikr. Remember Allah much — not just in prayer, but throughout your day. Make it a habit.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who only remembers Allah in prayer. Remember Allah much — throughout your day, in every situation.

arabic: وَمَا أَمْرُنَا إِلَّا وَاحِدَةٌ كَلَمْحٍ بِالْبَصَرِ
translation: And Our command is but one, like a glance of the eye.
tafsir: Allah's command is instant — like a blink of an eye. Nothing is difficult for Him. Creation is easy for Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your situation seems impossible. But for Allah, it is like a blink of an eye. Nothing is difficult for Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose situation seems impossible. For Allah, it is like a blink of an eye. Nothing is difficult for Him.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا
translation: O mankind, indeed the promise of Allah is truth, so let not the worldly life delude you.
tafsir: Allah's promise is true — do not be deceived by dunya. The worldly life is a delusion; the hereafter is the reality.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is being tested. Do not be deceived by dunya — Allah's promise is true. The hereafter is the reality.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone deceived by worldly life. Allah's promise is true — do not be deluded by dunya.

arabic: وَهُمْ يَصْطَرِخُونَ فِيهَا رَبَّنَا أَخْرِجْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَالِحًا غَيْرَ الَّذِي كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ
translation: And they will cry out therein: Our Lord, remove us; we will do righteousness other than what we used to do.
tafsir: In Hellfire, people will cry out: let us out, we will do good now. But it will be too late. Repent before it is too late.
reflectionTemplate: Your repentance needs urgency. In the hereafter, people will cry for another chance — but it will be too late. Repent now.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone delaying repentance. In Hellfire, people will cry for another chance — but it will be too late. Repent now.

arabic: أَمَّنْ هُوَ قَانِتٌ آنَاءَ اللَّيْلِ سَاجِدًا وَقَائِمًا يَحْذَرُ الْآخِرَةَ وَيَرْجُو رَحْمَةَ رَبِّهِ
translation: Is one who is devoutly obedient during periods of the night, prostrating and standing, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord?
tafsir: The best person: one who worships at night, prostrating, standing, fearing the hereafter, hoping for mercy. Fear + hope together.
reflectionTemplate: Your worship needs this balance. Be devout at night, prostrating, standing — fear the hereafter AND hope for mercy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking spiritual excellence. The best: worship at night, fear the hereafter, hope for mercy.

arabic: قُلْ يَا عِبَادِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا فِي هَٰذِهِ الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ
translation: Say: O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good.
tafsir: Allah addresses believing servants — fear Him and do good. Good in this world leads to good in the hereafter.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is the foundation. Fear Allah and do good — the reward is good in this life and the next.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking a good life. The formula: fear Allah and do good — good will come to you.

arabic: وَالْأَرْضَ بَسَطَهَا لِلْأَنْعَامِ
translation: And the earth He spread for the creatures.
tafsir: Allah spread the earth for creatures — provision is built into creation. Trust that He provides for you too.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq anxiety needs this perspective. Allah spread the earth for creatures — provision is built into creation. He provides for you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone anxious about provision. Allah built provision into creation — He spread the earth for creatures. He provides.

arabic: قُلْ يَا عِبَادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا مِن رَّحْمَةِ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعًا
translation: Say: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins.
tafsir: Allah forgives ALL sins — not some, but all. Do not despair no matter what you have done. His mercy encompasses everything.
reflectionTemplate: You feel you have gone too far. But Allah forgives ALL sins — not some, all. Do not despair. Turn back to Him now.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they are too sinful. Allah forgives ALL sins — not some, all. Do not despair.

arabic: غَافِرِ الذَّنبِ وَقَابِلِ التَّوْبِ شَدِيدِ الْعِقَابِ ذِي الطَّوْلِ
translation: The forgiver of sin, acceptor of repentance, severe in punishment, owner of abundance.
tafsir: Allah is: Ghafir adh-dhanb (Forgiver of sin), Qabir at-tawb (Accepter of repentance), Shadeed al-iqab (Severe in punishment), Dh at-tawl (Owner of abundance).
reflectionTemplate: Your repentance will be accepted. Allah is the Forgiver of sin and Accepter of repentance. Turn to Him — He accepts all repentance.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone hesitant to repent. Allah is the Forgiver of sin and Accepter of repentance. Turn to Him.

arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ
translation: Indeed, those who have said, 'Our Lord is Allah' and then remained on a right course — the angels will descend upon them.
tafsir: Those who say 'Our Lord is Allah' and remain steadfast — angels descend on them with glad tidings. Steadfastness brings angelic support.
reflectionTemplate: Your steadfastness brings angelic support. Say 'Rabbuna Allah' and remain firm — angels will descend with glad tidings.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking divine support. Say 'Rabbuna Allah' and remain steadfast — angels will descend on you.

arabic: وَمَا يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا وَمَا يُلَقَّاهَا إِلَّا ذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ
translation: And none are granted it except the patient, and none are granted it except one having a great portion of good fortune.
tafsir: The highest ranks in Paradise are given only to the patient — and only those with great fortune. Patience is the key to the highest ranks.
reflectionTemplate: Your patience leads to the highest ranks. Only the patient reach the top — and only those with great fortune. Keep being patient.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking the highest ranks. Only the patient reach the top — patience is the key to the highest Paradise.

arabic: اللَّهُ لَطِيفٌ بِعِبَادِهِ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَاءُ ۖ وَهُوَ الْقَوِيُّ الْعَزِيزُ
translation: Allah is Subtle with His servants; He provides for whom He wills. And He is the Powerful, the Exalted in Might.
tafsir: Allah is LATEEF (Subtle, Gentle) with His servants. He provides in ways you do not expect. Trust His subtle care.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq comes from Allah's subtleness. He provides in ways you do not expect — He is Lateef. Trust His gentle care.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone anxious about provision. Allah is Subtle with His servants — He provides in ways you do not expect.

arabic: وَيَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَيَزِيدُهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِ
translation: And He responds to those who believe and do righteous deeds and increases them from His bounty.
tafsir: Allah responds to believers who do good — and increases them from His bounty. Faith + action = response + increase.
reflectionTemplate: Your duas are being answered. Believe and do good — Allah responds and increases you from His bounty.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose duas feel unanswered. Believe and do good — Allah responds and increases you from His bounty.

arabic: وَالَّذِي نَزَّلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً بِقَدَرٍ فَأَنشَرْنَا بِهِ بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا
translation: And who sends down rain from the sky in measured amounts, and We revive thereby a dead land.
tafsir: Allah sends rain in measure and revives dead land. He can revive your dead situation too — trust His power.
reflectionTemplate: Your situation seems dead. But Allah revives dead land with measured rain — He can revive your situation too.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose situation seems hopeless. Allah revives dead land — He can revive your situation too.

arabic: لَا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا الْمَوْتَ إِلَّا الْمَوْتَةَ الْأُولَىٰ ۖ وَوَقَاهُمْ عَذَابَ الْجَحِيمِ
translation: They will not taste death therein except the first death, and He will have protected them from the punishment of Hellfire.
tafsir: In Jannah: no death except the first one, and protection from Hellfire. The ultimate safety and immortality.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this vision. In Jannah: no more death, no punishment. Just eternal bliss. Keep striving.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone fearing death. In Jannah: no more death, no punishment. Just eternal bliss. Keep striving.

arabic: وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ إِحْسَانًا ۖ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ كُرْهًا وَوَضَعَتْهُ كُرْهًا
translation: And We have enjoined upon man goodness to his parents. His mother carried him with hardship and gave birth to him with hardship.
tafsir: Allah commands goodness to parents — your mother carried you and bore you with hardship. Honor them always.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen includes your parents. Your mother carried you with hardship — honor her and your father always.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone neglecting their parents. Your mother carried you with hardship — honor her and your father always.

arabic: هُوَ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ السَّكِينَةَ فِي قُلُوبِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لِيَزْدَادُوا إِيمَانًا مَّعَ إِيمَانِهِمْ
translation: It is He who sent down tranquility into the hearts of the believers that they would increase in faith along with their faith.
tafsir: Allah sends tranquility (sakeenah) into believers' hearts — and their faith increases. Sakeenah comes from Allah alone.
reflectionTemplate: Your restless heart needs sakeenah. Ask Allah to send tranquility into your heart — and your faith will increase.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone with a restless heart. Ask Allah for sakeenah — He sends tranquility into believers' hearts.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَاكُم مِّن ذَكَرٍ وَأُنثَىٰ وَجَعَلْنَاكُمْ شُعُوبًا وَقَبَائِلَ لِتَعَارَفُوا
translation: O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another.
tafsir: Allah created diversity so we may know each other — not despise each other. The best in Allah's sight: most righteous.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs this perspective. Diversity is from Allah — to know each other, not despise. The best: most righteous.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who judges by race or tribe. Allah created diversity to know each other — the best is most righteous.

arabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
translation: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
tafsir: The purpose of existence is worship. Everything else — career, family, wealth — is the means, not the end.
reflectionTemplate: Your purpose is worship. Everything else is means, not ends. Remember why you were created — to worship Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone searching for purpose. You were created to worship Allah — everything else is secondary.

arabic: وَاصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ فَإِنَّكَ بِأَعْيُنِنَا
translation: And be patient for the decision of your Lord, for indeed, you are in Our eyes.
tafsir: Allah tells the Prophet ﷺ: be patient, you are in Our eyes. You are always under Allah's watchful care.
reflectionTemplate: You are under Allah's watchful care. Be patient — you are in His eyes. He sees you, protects you, and cares for you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling abandoned. Be patient — you are in Allah's eyes. He sees and cares for you.

arabic: إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَنَهَرٍ فِي مَقْعَدِ صِدْقٍ عِندَ مَلِيكٍ مُّقْتَدِرٍ
translation: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and rivers, in a seat of truth near a Powerful King.
tafsir: The righteous: in gardens, rivers, sitting in truth near the Powerful King. The ultimate honor and proximity to Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa leads to the ultimate honor: gardens, rivers, sitting near the Powerful King. Keep your taqwa strong.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking honor. The righteous sit near the Powerful King — in gardens and rivers. Keep your taqwa.

arabic: فَبِأَيِّ آلَاءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ
translation: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
tafsir: Allah asks repeatedly: which of His favors will you deny? Every blessing is from Him — acknowledge and be grateful.
reflectionTemplate: Your gratitude needs this question. Which of Allah's favors will you deny? Every blessing is from Him — be grateful.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone ungrateful. Which of Allah's favors will you deny? Every blessing is from Him — be grateful.

arabic: وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ
translation: And He is with you wherever you are.
tafsir: Allah is with you wherever you are — not physically, but in knowledge, hearing, seeing, and support.
reflectionTemplate: You feel alone. But Allah is with you wherever you are — He hears, sees, and supports you. You are never alone.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling alone. Allah is with you wherever you are — He hears, sees, and supports you.

arabic: وَلِكُلٍّ دَرَجَاتٌ مِّمَّا عَمِلُوا ۖ وَلِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
translation: And for all are degrees from what they have done. And He will fully repay them for their deeds.
tafsir: Everyone gets degrees based on their deeds — and full repayment. Nothing is wasted; everything is accounted for.
reflectionTemplate: Your deeds are not wasted. Everyone gets degrees based on what they do — and full repayment. Keep doing good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose effort feels wasted. Everyone gets degrees based on deeds — nothing is wasted. Keep doing good.

arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ
translation: Have you not considered that Allah knows what is in the heavens and what is on the earth?
tafsir: Allah knows everything in the heavens and earth — nothing is hidden from Him. Your secrets and your public acts.
reflectionTemplate: Allah knows everything — in the heavens and on earth. Nothing is hidden. Be honest with yourself and with Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone thinking they can hide. Allah knows everything in the heavens and earth — nothing is hidden.

arabic: هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ
translation: He is Allah, other than whom there is no deity, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed.
tafsir: Allah is the Knower of the unseen and witnessed — He knows everything, hidden and apparent.
reflectionTemplate: Your tawheed needs this truth. Allah knows the unseen and witnessed — turn to the All-Knowing.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking knowledge. Allah knows the unseen and witnessed — the All-Knowing.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا كَأَنَّهُم بُنْيَانٌ مَّرْصُوصٌ
translation: Indeed, Allah loves those who fight in His cause in a row as though they are a structure joined firmly.
tafsir: Allah loves those who strive in His way, standing firm like a solid structure. Unity and determination in deen.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah needs unity and determination. Stand firm like a solid structure — Allah loves those who strive in His way.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking strength in deen. Stand firm like a solid structure — Allah loves those who strive in His way.

arabic: فَإِذَا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلَاةُ فَانتَشِرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِن فَضْلِ اللَّهِ
translation: And when the prayer has been concluded, disperse within the land and seek from the bounty of Allah.
tafsir: After prayer: seek Allah's bounty. Prayer is not separate from worldly life — it energizes your pursuit of provision.
reflectionTemplate: Your time management needs this balance. Pray first, then seek Allah's bounty. Prayer energizes your worldly pursuits.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who separates prayer from work. Pray first, then seek bounty — prayer energizes your pursuits.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُلْهِكُمْ أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ
translation: O you who have believed, let not your wealth and your children divert you from the remembrance of Allah.
tafsir: Wealth and children should not divert you from dhikr. Do not let dunya distract you from remembering Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your dunya distractions are pulling you from dhikr. Do not let wealth and children divert you from remembering Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone too busy for Allah. Do not let wealth and children divert you from the remembrance of Allah.

arabic: مَا أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُ
translation: No disaster strikes except by the permission of Allah. And whoever believes in Allah — He will guide his heart.
tafsir: Everything happens by Allah's permission. Faith in this truth guides the heart through any difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty is by Allah's permission. Believe in this — and He will guide your heart through it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in difficulty. Everything is by Allah's permission — believe and He will guide your heart.

arabic: وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا
translation: And whoever fears Allah — He will make for him a way out.
tafsir: Taqwa leads to a way out of every difficulty. Fear Allah — and He will open doors you never expected.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty has a way out. Fear Allah — and He will make a way out for you. Taqwa is the key.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone trapped in difficulty. Fear Allah — He will make a way out. Taqwa is the key.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ تَوْبَةً نَّصُوحًا
translation: O you who have believed, repent to Allah with sincere repentance.
tafsir: Sincere repentance (tawbah nasuh) — the kind that changes you permanently. Turn back to Allah with everything you have.
reflectionTemplate: Your repentance needs sincerity. Turn back to Allah with sincere repentance — the kind that changes you permanently.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone needing to repent. Sincere repentance — tawbah nasuh — changes you permanently. Turn back to Allah.

arabic: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَامْشُوا فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا مِن رِّزْقِهِ
translation: It is He who made the earth tame for you, so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision.
tafsir: The earth is made for you — walk, explore, eat from Allah's provision. He created it all for your benefit.
reflectionTemplate: Your health barakah is low but the earth is made for you. Walk, move, explore — your body is an amanah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone trapped by health issues. The earth is made for you — walk and eat from His provision.

arabic: فَقُلْتُ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ إِنَّهُ كَانَ غَفَّارًا
translation: And said, 'Ask forgiveness of your Lord. Indeed, He is ever a Perpetual Forgiver.'
tafsir: Nuh ﷺ told his people: seek forgiveness. Allah is a Perpetual Forgiver — He never tires of forgiving.
reflectionTemplate: Your repentance will be accepted. Allah is a Perpetual Forgiver — He never tires of forgiving. Seek forgiveness now.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone hesitant to repent. Allah is a Perpetual Forgiver — He never tires of forgiving. Seek forgiveness now.

arabic: وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ وَآتِ الزَّكَاةَ وَأَقْرِضُوا اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا
translation: And establish prayer and give zakah and loan Allah a goodly loan.
tafsir: Prayer + zakah + lending to Allah (charity). These three together bring immense reward and barakah.
reflectionTemplate: Your spiritual life needs these three: prayer, zakah, and charity. Do all three — and watch your barakah increase.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking barakah. Three keys: prayer, zakah, and charity. Do all three — barakah will increase.

arabic: وَيُطْعِمُونَ الطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِ مِسْكِينًا وَيَتِيمًا وَأَسِيرًا إِنَّمَا نُطْعِمُكُمْ لِوَجْهِ اللَّهِ لَا نُرِيدُ مِنكُمْ جَزَاءً وَلَا شُكُورًا
translation: And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive. We feed you only for the countenance of Allah. We wish not from you reward or gratitude.
tafsir: The righteous feed the needy, orphans, and captives for Allah's sake alone — no reward, no thanks expected. Selfless giving.
reflectionTemplate: Your generosity needs selflessness. Give for Allah's sake alone — no reward, no thanks expected. This is true charity.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking true charity. Give for Allah's sake alone — no reward, no thanks expected. Selfless giving.

arabic: وَإِذَا الْمَوْءُودَةُ سُئِلَتْ بِأَيِّ ذَنبٍ قُتِلَتْ
translation: And when the infant girl buried alive is asked for what sin she was killed.
tafsir: On Judgment Day, even the buried girl will be asked about her murder. Every injustice will be accounted for.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception needs this truth. Every injustice will be accounted for — even the buried girl will be asked. Trust Allah's justice.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone questioning injustice. Every injustice will be accounted for — Allah's justice is absolute.

arabic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّىٰ
translation: He has certainly succeeded who purifies himself and remembers the name of his Lord and prays.
tafsir: Success is: purification + dhikr + prayer. These three together guarantee success in this life and the next.
reflectionTemplate: Your success formula: purify yourself, remember Allah, and pray. These three guarantee success.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking success. The formula: purify yourself, remember Allah, and pray. Guaranteed success.

arabic: يَا أَيَّتُهَا النَّفْسُ الْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ ارْجِعِي إِلَىٰ رَبِّكِ رَاضِيَةً مَّرْضِيَّةً فَادْخُلِي فِي عِبَادِي وَادْخُلِي جَنَّتِي
translation: O reassured soul, return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing to Him, and enter among My servants, and enter My Paradise.
tafsir: The ultimate goal: a soul at peace, returning to Allah pleased and pleasing Him, entering Paradise among His servants.
reflectionTemplate: Your spiritual journey leads here — a soul at peace, returning to Allah pleased. Strive for this ending: enter His Paradise.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking peace. The goal: a soul at peace, returning to Allah, entering Paradise. This is the ultimate success.

arabic: وَمَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ وَلَلْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لَّكَ مِنَ الْأُولَىٰ
translation: Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor has He become displeased. And the Hereafter is better for you than the first life.
tafsir: Allah has not abandoned you — the hereafter is better than this life. Whatever you are going through, the best is yet to come.
reflectionTemplate: You feel abandoned. But Allah has not forsaken you — the hereafter is better. The best is yet to come.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling abandoned. Allah has not forsaken you — the hereafter is better. The best is yet to come.

arabic: فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا
translation: For indeed, with hardship comes ease. Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
tafsir: Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. After every difficulty, ease is guaranteed. Hold on.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty is temporary. Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. Hold on. Ease is coming.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in difficulty. Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. Hold on. Ease is guaranteed.

arabic: لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنسَانَ فِي أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَاهُ أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ
translation: We have certainly created man in the best of stature. Then We return him to the lowest of the low. Except for those who believe and do righteous deeds.
tafsir: Humans were created in the best form — but can fall to the lowest. Except believers who do good — they maintain their honor.
reflectionTemplate: Your honor depends on faith and action. You were created in the best form — maintain it through belief and good deeds.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone losing their way. Created in the best form — but can fall. Except through faith and good deeds.

arabic: اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ خَلَقَ الْإِنسَانَ مِنْ عَلَقٍ اقْرَأْ وَرَبُّكَ الْأَكْرَمُ الَّذِي عَلَّمَ بِالْقَلَمِ عَلَّمَ الْإِنسَانَ مَا لَمْ يَعْلَمْ
translation: Read in the name of your Lord who created. Created man from a clinging substance. Read, and your Lord is the Most Generous. Who taught by the pen. Taught man that which he knew not.
tafsir: The first revelation: read! And your Lord is the Most Generous. He taught humans through the pen — knowledge is a divine gift.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm journey starts here. Read in the name of your Lord — the Most Generous. Knowledge is a divine gift.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking knowledge. Read in the name of your Lord — the Most Generous. Knowledge is a divine gift.

arabic: وَالْعَصْرِ إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ
translation: By time, indeed mankind is in loss. Except for those who have believed and done righteous deeds and advised each other to truth and advised each other to patience.
tafsir: Allah swears by time — mankind is in loss except: faith + good deeds + truth + patience. These four save you.
reflectionTemplate: Your salvation formula: faith + good deeds + truth + patience. Missing any one puts you in loss. Hold onto all four.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking salvation. The formula: faith + good deeds + truth + patience. These four save you.

arabic: إِنَّا أَعْطَيْنَاكَ الْكَوْثَرَ فَصَلِّ لِرَبِّكَ وَانْحَرْ إِنَّ شَانِئَكَ هُوَ الْأَبْتَرُ
translation: Indeed, We have granted you, O Muhammad, al-Kawthar. So pray to your Lord and sacrifice. Indeed, your enemy is the one cut off.
tafsir: Allah gave the Prophet ﷺ al-Kawthar (abundance). The response: pray and sacrifice. Your enemies are the ones cut off.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah courage needs this truth. Allah gave you abundance — pray and sacrifice. Your enemies are the ones cut off.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone afraid of enemies. Allah gives abundance to the believers — pray and sacrifice. Your enemies are cut off.

arabic: إِذَا جَاءَ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ وَالْفَتْحُ وَرَأَيْتَ النَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًا فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ
translation: When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest, and you see the people entering into the religion of Allah in multitudes, then exalt Him with praise of your Lord and ask forgiveness of Him.
tafsir: When victory comes: praise Allah and seek forgiveness. Success leads to humility, not arrogance.
reflectionTemplate: Your success should lead to humility. When Allah gives you victory, praise Him and seek forgiveness. Stay humble.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone experiencing success. When victory comes: praise Allah and seek forgiveness. Stay humble.

arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
translation: Say: He is Allah, the One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born. Nor is there to Him any equivalent.
tafsir: Surah Ikhlas — the essence of tawheed. Allah is One, the Eternal Refuge, without equal.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa needs this foundation. Allah is One, the Eternal Refuge — turn to Him alone.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking the essence of Islam. Allah is One, the Eternal Refuge — this is the foundation.

arabic: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ
translation: Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak. From the evil of that which He created. And from the evil of darkness when it settles. And from the evil of the blowers in knots. And from the evil of an envier when he envies.
tafsir: Surah Falaq — seeking refuge in Allah from all evil: creation, darkness, magic, and envy. Protection through Allah alone.
reflectionTemplate: Your protection comes from Allah. Seek refuge in Him from all evil — creation, darkness, magic, and envy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking protection. Surah Falaq: seek refuge in Allah from all evil. He alone protects.

arabic: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ مَلِكِ النَّاسِ إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
translation: Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind. The Sovereign of mankind. The God of mankind. From the evil of the retreating whisperer. Who whispers into the breasts of mankind. From among the jinn and mankind.
tafsir: Surah Nas — seeking refuge in Allah from the whisperer (Shaytan). Protection from evil whispers that enter the heart.
reflectionTemplate: Your nafs struggles need this protection. Seek refuge in Allah from the whisperer — He protects your heart from evil whispers.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with waswas. Surah Nas: seek refuge in Allah from the whisperer. He protects your heart.

arabic: فَمَن تَبِعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ
translation: And whoever follows My guidance — there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve. But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs — those are companions of the Fire.
tafsir: Following Allah's guidance = no fear, no grief. Denying His signs = Hellfire. The choice is clear.
reflectionTemplate: Follow Allah's guidance — no fear, no grief. The alternative is not worth considering. Stay on the path.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone hesitating about Islam. Follow Allah's guidance: no fear, no grief. The choice is clear.

arabic: بَلَىٰ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ فَلَهُ أَجْرُهُ عِندَ رَبِّهِ
translation: Yes, whoever submits his face to Allah while being a doer of good — he will have his reward with his Lord.
tafsir: Submission to Allah + doing good = reward from your Lord. The formula is simple and the reward is guaranteed.
reflectionTemplate: Your salvation is simple: submit to Allah and do good. The reward is guaranteed from your Lord.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone overcomplicating Islam. Submit to Allah and do good — the reward is guaranteed.

arabic: وَلِكُلٍّ وِجْهَةٌ هُوَ مُوَلِّيهَا ۖ فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْرَاتِ
translation: And for each direction to which He turns you, so compete in good deeds.
tafsir: Every person has a direction — compete in good deeds. The race is not for wealth but for goodness.
reflectionTemplate: Your life is a race — but for good deeds, not wealth. Compete in goodness with everyone around you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone competitive. Compete in good deeds — that is the race that matters.

arabic: لَّيْسَ الْبِرَّ أَن تُوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ قِبَلَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ وَلَٰكِنَّ الْبِرَّ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ
translation: Righteousness is not that you turn your faces toward the East or the West. But righteousness is one who believes in Allah, the Last Day.
tafsir: True righteousness is not rituals alone — it is faith in Allah, the Last Day, angels, books, prophets, and giving wealth.
reflectionTemplate: Your faith needs depth. Righteousness is not just rituals — it is complete faith and generous giving.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is only rituals. True righteousness: faith + charity + patience + truth.

arabic: شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ الَّذِي أُنزِلَ فِيهِ الْقُرْآنُ هُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَاتٍ مِّنَ الْهُدَىٰ وَالْفُرْقَانِ
translation: The month of Ramadan in which was revealed the Quran, a guidance for the people and clear proofs of guidance and criterion.
tafsir: Ramadan: the month the Quran was revealed — guidance, clear proofs, and criterion. A month of mercy and light.
reflectionTemplate: Your connection with the Quran needs Ramadan. The month of guidance and clear proofs — reconnect with the Quran.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking guidance. Ramadan: the month the Quran was revealed — guidance for all people.

arabic: وَأَنفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَلَا تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى التَّهْلُكَةِ
translation: And spend in the cause of Allah and do not put yourselves into destruction by your own hands.
tafsir: Spend in Allah's way — do not destroy yourselves by withholding. Generosity protects; stinginess destroys.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq barakah needs generosity. Spend in Allah's way — do not destroy yourself by withholding.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone afraid to give. Spend in Allah's way — generosity protects, stinginess destroys.

arabic: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
translation: And among them are those who say: Our Lord, give us in this world good and in the Hereafter good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
tafsir: The best dua: good in this world AND the hereafter, and protection from Hellfire. Balance between dunya and akhirah.
reflectionTemplate: Your dua life needs this balance. Ask for good in this world AND the hereafter — and protection from the Fire.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone learning to make dua. The best dua: good in this world and the hereafter, protection from the Fire.

arabic: أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَن تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِكُم مَّثَلُ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِن قَبْلِكُم
translation: Or do you think that you will enter Paradise while such trial has not yet came to you as came to those who passed on before you?
tafsir: Entering Paradise requires trials — just like those before you. Tests are part of the path to Paradise.
reflectionTemplate: Your trials are part of the path. Those before you were tested severely — and they entered Paradise. Your turn is coming.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone questioning their tests. Trials are part of the path to Paradise — those before you faced them too.

arabic: لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ
translation: Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear. It will have what it earned, and it will bear what it earned.
tafsir: Allah never overburdens anyone — what you earn is yours, what you bear is yours. You can handle what He gives you.
reflectionTemplate: Your burden is within your capacity. Allah does not overburden anyone — you can handle this. Trust His wisdom.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone overwhelmed. Allah does not overburden anyone — you can handle this. Trust His wisdom.

arabic: الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
translation: Those who say: Our Lord, indeed we have believed, so forgive us our sins and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
tafsir: The dua of the believers: we believed, forgive us, protect us from the Fire. Simple, powerful, and complete.
reflectionTemplate: Your dua needs simplicity. Say: Our Lord, we believed, forgive us, protect us from the Fire.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone learning to make dua. Simple and powerful: Our Lord, we believed, forgive us, protect us.

arabic: الصَّابِرِينَ وَالصَّادِقِينَ وَالْقَانِتِينَ وَالْمُنفِقِينَ وَالْمُسْتَغْفِرِينَ بِالْأَسْحَارِ
translation: The patient, the truthful, the obedient, those who spend, and those who seek forgiveness before dawn.
tafsir: The qualities of the righteous: patient, truthful, obedient, generous, seeking forgiveness at dawn. A complete spiritual profile.
reflectionTemplate: Your spiritual profile needs these qualities: patience, truthfulness, obedience, generosity, and seeking forgiveness at dawn.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking spiritual excellence. The qualities: patient, truthful, obedient, generous, seeking forgiveness at dawn.

arabic: وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَلَا تَفَرَّقُوا
translation: And hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and do not become divided.
tafsir: Hold firmly to Allah's rope — the Quran, Islam — and do not divide. Unity in faith is a command from Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs unity. Hold firmly to Allah's rope and do not divide. Unity is a command from Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone causing division. Hold firmly to Allah's rope and do not divide. Unity is commanded.

arabic: كُنتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ تَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ
translation: You are the best nation produced for mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong.
tafsir: The Muslim ummah is the best nation — because they enjoin good and forbid evil. This is your identity and responsibility.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah identity: you are the best nation. Enjoin good, forbid evil — this is your responsibility and honor.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone doubting the Muslim identity. You are the best nation — enjoin good, forbid evil.

arabic: وَلَا تَهِنُوا وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَنتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
translation: So do not weaken and do not grieve, and you will be superior if you are true believers.
tafsir: Believers are superior — do not weaken or grieve. Faith gives you the upper hand in every situation.
reflectionTemplate: You are feeling weak or sad. But Allah says you are superior — if you truly believe. Stand tall in your faith.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling defeated. Believers are superior — do not weaken or grieve. Stand tall.

arabic: فَبِمَا رَحْمَةٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ لِنتَ لَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ كُنتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ الْقَلْبِ لَانفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ
translation: So by mercy from Allah, you were lenient with them. And if you had been rude and harsh in heart, they would have dispersed from about you.
tafsir: Mercy and gentleness in dawah — harshness drives people away. The Prophet ﷺ was gentle by Allah's mercy.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah approach needs mercy. Be gentle — harshness drives people away. Mercy from Allah made the Prophet ﷺ successful.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone harsh in dawah. Be gentle — harshness drives people away. Mercy is the key.

arabic: إِنَّمَا التَّوْبَةُ عَلَى اللَّهِ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السُّوءَ بِجَهَالَةٍ ثُمَّ يَتُوبُونَ مِن قَرِيبٍ
translation: The repentance accepted by Allah is only for those who do wrong in ignorance and then repent soon after.
tafsir: Allah accepts repentance from those who sin in ignorance and repent soon after. Do not delay your repentance.
reflectionTemplate: Your repentance needs urgency. Repent soon after sinning — do not delay. Allah accepts repentance from those who turn quickly.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone delaying repentance. Repent soon after sinning — do not delay. Allah accepts quick repentance.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَن يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن يَشَاءُ
translation: Indeed, Allah does not forgive association with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whom He wills.
tafsir: Allah forgives everything except shirk. No matter what sin you have committed — if it is not shirk, Allah can forgive it.
reflectionTemplate: Your sins can be forgiven. Allah forgives everything except shirk — no matter how big the sin. Turn to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone thinking their sins are too big. Allah forgives everything except shirk — turn to Him.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تُؤَدُّوا الْأَمَانَاتِ إِلَىٰ أَهْلِهَا
translation: Indeed, Allah commands you to render trusts to their owners.
tafsir: Allah commands returning trusts to their owners — integrity and honesty are fundamental in Islam.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs integrity. Return trusts to their owners — honesty and integrity are commanded by Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with integrity. Return trusts to their owners — honesty is commanded by Allah.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ بِالْقِسْطِ شُهَدَاءَ لِلَّهِ
translation: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice, witnesses for Allah.
tafsir: Stand firm in justice — even against yourselves, parents, or relatives. Justice is a fundamental Islamic value.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs justice. Stand firm in justice — even against yourself or your family. Justice is fundamental.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone compromising justice. Stand firm in justice — even against yourself or family. Justice is fundamental.

arabic: وَتَعَاوَنُوا عَلَى الْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوَىٰ وَلَا تَعَاوَنُوا عَلَى الْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ
translation: And cooperate in righteousness and piety, but do not cooperate in sin and aggression.
tafsir: Cooperate in good — not in sin. Brotherhood means supporting each other in righteousness, not in wrongdoing.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs this principle. Cooperate in good, not in sin. Support others in righteousness.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in bad company. Cooperate in good, not in sin. Support others in righteousness.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَابْتَغُوا إِلَيْهِ الْوَسِيلَةَ
translation: O you who have believed, fear Allah and seek the means of nearness to Him.
tafsir: Fear Allah and seek closeness to Him — through prayer, dhikr, good deeds, and obedience. The path to Allah is open.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa needs a path. Fear Allah and seek closeness to Him — through prayer, dhikr, and good deeds.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking closeness to Allah. Fear Him and seek the means — prayer, dhikr, good deeds.

arabic: وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ اللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ فَلَا كَاشِفَ لَهُ إِلَّا هُوَ
translation: And if Allah should touch you with adversity, there is no remover of it except Him.
tafsir: Only Allah can remove harm. No doctor, no person, no plan — only Allah. Turn to Him in difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty can only be removed by Allah. Turn to Him — no one else can remove it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking help from others first. Only Allah can remove harm — turn to Him first.

arabic: وَمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۖ وَلَلدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ
translation: And the worldly life is not but amusement and diversion; but the home of the Hereafter is best for those who fear Allah.
tafsir: Dunya is play and amusement — the akhirah is the real home for those who have taqwa.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation is low. Dunya is play — the real home is the akhirah for those who fear Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone attached to this world. Dunya is play — the real home is the hereafter.

arabic: قُلْ مَن يُنَجِّيكُم مِّن ظُلُمَاتِ الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ تَدْعُونَهُ تَضَرُّعًا وَخُفْيَةً
translation: Say: Who saves you from the darknesses of the land and sea, when you call upon Him in humility and supplication?
tafsir: When in danger, everyone calls upon Allah. But why only in danger? Call upon Him always — in ease and difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your dua life needs consistency. Call upon Allah in ease AND difficulty — not just when you are in danger.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who only prays in crisis. Call upon Allah always — in ease and difficulty.

arabic: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا إِيمَانَهُم بِظُلْمٍ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمُ الْأَمْنُ وَهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ
translation: It is those who believe and do not mix their belief with injustice — those will have security, and they are guided.
tafsir: Faith without injustice = security and guidance. Do not mix your faith with oppression or wrongdoing.
reflectionTemplate: Your faith needs purity. Do not mix belief with injustice — those who keep faith pure have security and guidance.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone compromising their faith. Do not mix belief with injustice — pure faith brings security.

arabic: وَنَزَعْنَا مَا فِي صُدُورِهِم مِّنْ غِلٍّ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهَارُ
translation: And We will have removed whatever is within their breasts of resentment. Rivers will flow beneath them.
tafsir: In Jannah: all resentment removed from hearts, rivers flowing beneath. Complete peace and harmony.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this vision. In Jannah: all resentment removed, rivers flowing. Complete peace awaits.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone holding grudges. In Jannah: all resentment removed, rivers flowing. Let go now.

arabic: ادْعُوا رَبَّكُمْ تَضَرُّعًا وَخُفْيَةً ۚ إِنَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ
translation: Call upon your Lord in humility and privately. Indeed, He does not like transgressors.
tafsir: Make dua in humility and privately — not arrogantly or publicly for show. Allah loves humble, sincere supplication.
reflectionTemplate: Your dua needs humility. Call upon your Lord in humility and privately — not for show.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone making dua for show. Call upon Allah in humility and privately — He loves sincere supplication.

arabic: الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ الرَّسُولَ النَّبِيَّ الْأُمِّيَّ الَّذِي يَجِدُونَهُ مَكْتُوبًا عِندَهُمْ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنجِيلِ
translation: Those who follow the Messenger, the unlettered prophet, whom they find written in what they have of the Torah and the Gospel.
tafsir: Following the Prophet ﷺ is the path — he was foretold in previous scriptures. His way is the way to Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah has historical proof. The Prophet ﷺ was foretold in previous scriptures — following him is the path.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking proof. The Prophet ﷺ was foretold in previous scriptures — following him is the path.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اسْتَجِيبُوا لِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ لِمَا يُحْيِيكُمْ
translation: O you who have believed, respond to Allah and to the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life.
tafsir: Respond to Allah and His Messenger — they call you to LIFE. Islam is life; ignoring the call is spiritual death.
reflectionTemplate: Your response to Allah's call determines your spiritual life. Respond to Him — He calls you to life.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone ignoring Islam. Respond to Allah — He calls you to life. Ignoring is spiritual death.

arabic: وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتُ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ
translation: The believing men and believing women are allies of one another. They enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong.
tafsir: Believers are allies — supporting each other in good and preventing evil. Brotherhood in faith.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs alliance. Be an ally to believers — enjoin good, forbid evil. Support each other.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling isolated. Believers are allies — support each other in good.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَتْكُم مَّوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَشِفَاءٌ لِّمَا فِي الصُّدُورِ
translation: O mankind, there has come to you instruction from your Lord and healing for what is in the hearts.
tafsir: The Quran is healing for hearts — not just a book to read but medicine for the soul.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart needs healing. The Quran is the medicine — read it and let it heal what is in your chest.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone with a troubled heart. The Quran is healing for what is in the hearts.

arabic: وَيَا قَوْمِ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيْهِ
translation: And O my people, ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him.
tafsir: Hud ﷺ told his people: seek forgiveness and repent. The universal message: turn back to Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your repentance is simple: seek forgiveness and repent. The prophets all said the same thing: turn back to Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone needing to repent. Seek forgiveness and repent — the universal message of all prophets.

arabic: وَلَأَجْرُ الْآخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكَانُوا يَتَّقُونَ
translation: And the reward of the Hereafter is better for those who believed and were fearing Allah.
tafsir: The akhirah reward is better for those who believe and have taqwa. The best is yet to come.
reflectionTemplate: Your reward in the hereafter is better — if you believe and fear Allah. The best is yet to come.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone needing motivation. The reward of the hereafter is better for those who believe and fear Allah.

arabic: أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ
translation: Verily, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest.
tafsir: Dhikr is the medicine for restless hearts. True peace comes from remembering Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless. Return to dhikr — in the remembrance of Allah, hearts find rest.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking peace. In the remembrance of Allah, hearts find rest.

arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً كَشَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ أَصْلُهَا ثَابِتٌ وَفَرْعُهَا فِي السَّمَاءِ
translation: Have you not considered how Allah presents an example? A good word is like a good tree, whose root is firmly fixed and its branches in the sky.
tafsir: A good word (faith, dhikr, dawah) is like a tree — firmly rooted, branches in heaven, always producing fruit.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah and dhikr are like a tree — rooted in faith, branches in heaven. Keep planting good words.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking lasting impact. A good word is like a tree — rooted firmly, branches in heaven.

arabic: نَبِّئْ عِبَادِي أَنِّي أَنَا الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
translation: Inform My servants that I am the Forgiving, the Merciful.
tafsir: Allah announces His forgiveness and mercy. This is the message: Allah forgives and has mercy.
reflectionTemplate: You need to hear this: Allah is Al-Ghafoor, Ar-Raheem. He forgives and has mercy — turn to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who feels unforgiven. Allah says: I am the Forgiving, the Merciful.

arabic: مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً
translation: Whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while being a believer — We will surely cause them to live a good life.
tafsir: Faith + righteous deeds = a good life (hayatan tayyibah). The promise of a good life is tied to faith and action.
reflectionTemplate: Your good life comes from faith and action. Do righteous deeds while believing — Allah promises a good life.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking a good life. Faith + righteous deeds = a good life. Allah promises it.

arabic: وَقُل رَّبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
translation: And say: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up when I was small.
tafsir: The dua for parents — asking Allah to have mercy on them as they raised you.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen includes your parents. Make this dua: My Lord, have mercy on them as they raised me.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose parents need dua. My Lord, have mercy on them as they raised me when I was small.

arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ الْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلًا
translation: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds — they will have the Gardens of Paradise as a lodging.
tafsir: Faith + righteous deeds = Gardens of Paradise. The ultimate reward.
reflectionTemplate: Your path leads to Paradise — faith + righteous deeds. Keep believing and doing good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking motivation. Faith + righteous deeds = Gardens of Paradise.

arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي وَاحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِي يَفْقَهُوا قَوْلِي
translation: My Lord, expand for me my breast and ease for me my task and untie the knot from my tongue that they may understand my speech.
tafsir: The dua of Musa ﷺ — for confidence, ease, and clear speech. The ultimate dua for dawah courage.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah readiness needs this dua. Ask Allah: expand my chest, ease my task, loosen my tongue.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone afraid to speak about Islam. The dua of Musa: expand my chest, ease my task, loosen my tongue.

arabic: وَأَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ الضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
translation: And Job, when he called out to his Lord: Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful.
tafsir: Ayyub ﷺ called upon Allah in his illness. Allah healed him. Turn to the Most Merciful in your illness.
reflectionTemplate: You are suffering. Call upon Allah like Ayyub: adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in illness. Ayyub's dua: adversity has touched me, You are the Most Merciful. Allah healed him.

arabic: وَجَاهِدُوا فِي اللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ ۚ هُوَ اجْتَبَاكُمْ وَمَا جَعَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ مِنْ حَرَجٍ
translation: And strive for Allah with the striving due to Him. He has chosen you and has not placed upon you in the religion any difficulty.
tafsir: Strive for Allah — He chose you and made religion easy. There is no hardship in Islam.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah needs this truth. Allah chose you and made Islam easy — strive with what you can.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone finding Islam difficult. Allah made it easy — strive with what you can.

arabic: وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ
translation: Allah has promised those who have believed among you and done righteous deeds that He will surely grant them succession upon the earth.
tafsir: Allah promises believers who do good: succession on earth. Faith + action = authority and honor.
reflectionTemplate: Your faith and action lead to honor. Allah promises succession on earth for believers who do good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking honor. Allah promises believers who do good: succession on earth.

arabic: وَعِبَادُ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ هَوْنًا
translation: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth humbly.
tafsir: The servants of Ar-Rahman walk humbly on earth — not arrogantly. Humility is the mark of the righteous.
reflectionTemplate: Your character needs humility. Walk humbly on earth — the servants of Ar-Rahman are humble.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with arrogance. The servants of Ar-Rahman walk humbly on earth.

arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَالِدَيَّ
translation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents.
tafsir: The dua of Sulayman ﷺ — asking for ability to be grateful. Gratitude itself is a gift from Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your gratitude needs divine help. Ask like Sulayman: My Lord, enable me to be grateful.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone blessed. Gratitude is a gift — ask Allah to enable you to be grateful.

arabic: وَمَن جَاهَدَ فَإِنَّمَا يُجَاهِدُ لِنَفْسِهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَغَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعَالَمِينَ
translation: And whoever strives — strives only for himself. Indeed, Allah is free from need of the worlds.
tafsir: Your striving benefits YOU, not Allah. He does not need you — you need Him. Strive for your own benefit.
reflectionTemplate: Your effort benefits you, not Allah. He is free from need — strive for your own salvation.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose effort feels wasted. Your striving benefits you — Allah does not need you.

arabic: يَا بُنَيَّ أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَانْهَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَاصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا أَصَابَكَ
translation: O my son, establish prayer, enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and be patient over what afflicts you.
tafsir: Luqman's advice: pray, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient. The complete formula for a successful life.
reflectionTemplate: Your life needs this formula: pray, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient. Follow Luqman's advice.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking life advice. Luqman's wisdom: pray, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient.

arabic: إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْلِمَاتِ وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَالْقَانِتِينَ وَالْقَانِتَاتِ
translation: Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women, the believing men and believing women, the obedient men and obedient women.
tafsir: Allah mentions men and women equally in faith and obedience. Both genders are equal in reward and responsibility.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah includes everyone. Men and women are equal in faith and obedience — invite all to Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone questioning gender in Islam. Men and women are equal in faith and obedience before Allah.

arabic: مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ الْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعًا
translation: Whoever desires honor — then to Allah belongs all honor.
tafsir: True honor comes from Allah alone — not from wealth, status, or people.
reflectionTemplate: Your honor comes from Allah — not from people. Seek honor through faith, not approval.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking approval from people. True honor is from Allah — seek it through faith.

arabic: إِنَّمَا أَمْرُهُ إِذَا أَرَادَ شَيْئًا أَن يَقُولَ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
translation: His command is only when He intends a thing that He says to it, 'Be,' and it is.
tafsir: Allah's power is absolute — He creates by saying 'Be.' Nothing is difficult for Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your situation seems impossible. But for Allah, nothing is impossible — He says 'Be' and it is.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone whose situation seems impossible. For Allah, nothing is impossible — He says 'Be' and it is.

arabic: قُلْ يَا عِبَادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا مِن رَّحْمَةِ اللَّهِ
translation: Say: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah.
tafsir: Allah addresses sinners directly — do not despair. His mercy is greater than any sin.
reflectionTemplate: You feel you have gone too far. But Allah says: do not despair of My mercy. Turn back to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks they are too sinful. Allah says: do not despair of My mercy.

arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ
translation: Indeed, those who have said, 'Our Lord is Allah' and then remained on a right course — the angels will descend upon them.
tafsir: Those who say 'Rabbuna Allah' and remain steadfast — angels descend with glad tidings.
reflectionTemplate: Your steadfastness brings angelic support. Say 'Rabbuna Allah' and remain firm.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking divine support. Say 'Rabbuna Allah' and remain steadfast.

arabic: اللَّهُ لَطِيفٌ بِعِبَادِهِ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَاءُ
translation: Allah is Subtle with His servants; He provides for whom He wills.
tafsir: Allah is LATEEF (Subtle, Gentle) with His servants. He provides in ways you do not expect.
reflectionTemplate: Your rizq comes from Allah's subtleness. He provides in ways you do not expect — trust His gentle care.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone anxious about provision. Allah is Subtle — He provides in ways you do not expect.

arabic: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَعْلَمَ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ مِنكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ
translation: And We will surely test you until We make evident those who strive among you and the patient.
tafsir: Tests reveal who truly strives and who is patient. Allah tests to elevate, not to punish.
reflectionTemplate: You are being tested. This test reveals your true level — strive and be patient.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling tested. Tests reveal the mujahid and the patient — be among them.

arabic: إِنَّا فَتَحْنَا لَكَ فَتْحًا مُّبِينًا
translation: Indeed, We have given you a clear conquest.
tafsir: Allah gave the Prophet ﷺ a clear victory — after years of struggle. Victory comes after patience.
reflectionTemplate: Your patience will lead to clear victory. After struggle comes conquest — Allah promised it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling. After struggle comes clear victory — Allah promised it.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اجْتَنِبُوا كَثِيرًا مِّنَ الظَّنِّ إِنَّ بَعْضَ الظَّنِّ إِثْمٌ
translation: O you who have believed, avoid much suspicion. Indeed, some suspicion is sin.
tafsir: Avoid negative assumptions about people — some suspicion is sin. Think well of others.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen needs better assumptions. Avoid negative thinking about others — some suspicion is sin.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who assumes the worst. Avoid much suspicion — some of it is sin.

arabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
translation: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.
tafsir: The purpose of existence is worship. Everything else is means, not ends.
reflectionTemplate: Your purpose is worship. Remember why you were created — to worship Allah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone searching for purpose. You were created to worship Allah.

arabic: وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ
translation: And He is with you wherever you are.
tafsir: Allah is with you wherever you are — in knowledge, hearing, seeing, and support.
reflectionTemplate: You feel alone. But Allah is with you wherever you are. You are never alone.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling alone. Allah is with you wherever you are.

arabic: هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ
translation: He is Allah, other than whom there is no deity, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed.
tafsir: Allah knows the unseen and witnessed — turn to the All-Knowing.
reflectionTemplate: Your tawheed needs this truth. Allah knows the unseen and witnessed — turn to Him.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking knowledge. Allah knows the unseen and witnessed.

arabic: مَا أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُ
translation: No disaster strikes except by the permission of Allah. And whoever believes in Allah — He will guide his heart.
tafsir: Everything happens by Allah's permission. Faith guides the heart through difficulty.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty is by Allah's permission. Believe — and He will guide your heart.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in difficulty. Everything is by Allah's permission — believe and He will guide your heart.

arabic: وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ
translation: And whoever fears Allah — He will make for him a way out. And will provide for him from where he does not expect.
tafsir: Taqwa leads to a way out and unexpected provision. Fear Allah — and doors will open.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty has a way out. Fear Allah — He will make a way and provide unexpectedly.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone trapped. Fear Allah — He will make a way out and provide unexpectedly.

arabic: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَامْشُوا فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا مِن رِّزْقِهِ
translation: It is He who made the earth tame for you, so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision.
tafsir: The earth is made for you — walk, explore, eat from Allah's provision.
reflectionTemplate: Your health barakah is low but the earth is made for you. Walk, move, explore.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone trapped by health issues. The earth is made for you — walk and eat from His provision.

arabic: فَقُلْتُ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ إِنَّهُ كَانَ غَفَّارًا
translation: And said, 'Ask forgiveness of your Lord. Indeed, He is ever a Perpetual Forgiver.'
tafsir: Seek forgiveness — Allah is a Perpetual Forgiver. He never tires of forgiving.
reflectionTemplate: Your repentance will be accepted. Allah is a Perpetual Forgiver — seek forgiveness now.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone hesitant to repent. Allah is a Perpetual Forgiver — seek forgiveness now.

arabic: وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ وَآتِ الزَّكَاةَ وَأَقْرِضُوا اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا
translation: And establish prayer and give zakah and loan Allah a goodly loan.
tafsir: Prayer + zakah + charity. These three together bring immense reward and barakah.
reflectionTemplate: Your spiritual life needs these three: prayer, zakah, and charity. Do all three — barakah will increase.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking barakah. Three keys: prayer, zakah, and charity.

arabic: وَيُطْعِمُونَ الطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِ مِسْكِينًا وَيَتِيمًا وَأَسِيرًا إِنَّمَا نُطْعِمُكُمْ لِوَجْهِ اللَّهِ
translation: And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive. We feed you only for the countenance of Allah.
tafsir: The righteous feed the needy for Allah's sake alone — selfless giving.
reflectionTemplate: Your generosity needs selflessness. Give for Allah's sake alone — no reward expected.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking true charity. Give for Allah's sake alone — selfless giving.

arabic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ وَذَكَرَ اسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّىٰ
translation: He has certainly succeeded who purifies himself and remembers the name of his Lord and prays.
tafsir: Success: purification + dhikr + prayer. These three guarantee success.
reflectionTemplate: Your success formula: purify yourself, remember Allah, and pray.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking success. The formula: purify, remember Allah, pray.

arabic: يَا أَيَّتُهَا النَّفْسُ الْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ ارْجِعِي إِلَىٰ رَبِّكِ رَاضِيَةً مَّرْضِيَّةً فَادْخُلِي فِي عِبَادِي وَادْخُلِي جَنَّتِي
translation: O reassured soul, return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing to Him, and enter among My servants, and enter My Paradise.
