tafsir: The ultimate goal: a soul at peace, returning to Allah pleased and pleasing Him, entering Paradise.
reflectionTemplate: Your journey leads here — a soul at peace, entering Paradise. Strive for this ending.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking peace. The goal: a soul at peace, entering Paradise.

arabic: فَإِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا
translation: For indeed, with hardship comes ease. Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
tafsir: Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. After every difficulty, ease is guaranteed.
reflectionTemplate: Your difficulty is temporary. Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. Hold on.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in difficulty. Allah promises ease — not once, but twice. Hold on.

arabic: وَالْعَصْرِ إِنَّ الْإِنسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ
translation: By time, indeed mankind is in loss. Except for those who have believed and done righteous deeds and advised each other to truth and patience.
tafsir: Faith + good deeds + truth + patience. These four save you from loss.
reflectionTemplate: Your salvation formula: faith + good deeds + truth + patience.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking salvation. The formula: faith + good deeds + truth + patience.

arabic: إِذَا جَاءَ نَصْرُ اللَّهِ وَالْفَتْحُ فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ
translation: When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest, then exalt Him with praise of your Lord and ask forgiveness.
tafsir: When victory comes: praise Allah and seek forgiveness. Success leads to humility.
reflectionTemplate: Your success should lead to humility. Praise Allah and seek forgiveness in victory.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone experiencing success. When victory comes: praise Allah and seek forgiveness.

arabic: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَيْءٍ مِّنَ الْخَوْفِ وَالْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَنفُسِ وَالثَّمَرَاتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ الَّذِينَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
translation: And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient who, when disaster strikes, say: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return.
tafsir: Tests are guaranteed — fear, hunger, loss. But the patient say 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' and receive glad tidings.
reflectionTemplate: You are being tested. Respond like the patient: 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un.' Glad tidings await you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in grief. The response: 'We belong to Allah and to Him we return.' Glad tidings for the patient.

arabic: الَّذِينَ قَالَ لَهُمُ النَّاسُ إِنَّ النَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا لَكُمْ فَاخْشَوْهُمْ فَزَادَهُمْ إِيمَانًا وَقَالُوا حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
translation: Those to whom people said: Indeed, the people have gathered against you, so fear them. But it increased them in faith, and they said: Sufficient for us is Allah, and He is the best Disposer of affairs.
tafsir: When enemies gathered, their faith INCREASED. They said 'Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal wakeel' — and Allah proved sufficient.
reflectionTemplate: You face opposition. Let it increase your faith. Say: Allah is sufficient for me and the best disposer of affairs.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone facing opposition. Say 'Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal wakeel' — and watch your faith increase.

arabic: كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ ۗ وَإِنَّمَا تُوَفَّوْنَ أُجُورَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
translation: Every soul will taste death. And you will only be given your full compensation on the Day of Resurrection.
tafsir: Death is certain — the real account is on Judgment Day. This life is temporary; the akhirah is forever.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this reminder. Death is certain — prepare for the real life.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone living as if they will never die. Death is certain — prepare for the real life.

arabic: يَهْدِي بِهِ اللَّهُ مَنِ اتَّبَعَ رِضْوَانَهُ سُبُلَ السَّلَامِ
translation: By which Allah guides those who pursue His pleasure to the ways of peace.
tafsir: Allah guides those who seek His pleasure to the ways of peace. Seeking Allah's pleasure = finding peace.
reflectionTemplate: Your guidance comes from seeking Allah's pleasure. Pursue His pleasure — and He will guide you to peace.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking guidance. Seek Allah's pleasure — He will guide you to the ways of peace.

arabic: إِنَّ رَحْمَتَ اللَّهِ قَرِيبٌ مِّنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
translation: Indeed, the mercy of Allah is near to the doers of good.
tafsir: Allah's mercy is near those who do good. Do good — and His mercy will be close to you.
reflectionTemplate: Your mercy from Allah depends on your actions. Do good — and His mercy will be near.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking Allah's mercy. The mercy of Allah is near the doers of good.

arabic: وَلِلَّهِ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَىٰ فَادْعُوهُ بِهَا
translation: And to Allah belong the best names, so invoke Him by them.
tafsir: Allah has the most beautiful names — call upon Him using them. Each name reveals an attribute.
reflectionTemplate: Your dua needs depth. Call upon Allah by His beautiful names — Ar-Rahman, Ar-Raheem, Al-Ghafoor.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone learning to make dua. Call upon Allah by His beautiful names.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ اشْتَرَىٰ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَالَهُم بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ
translation: Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their properties in exchange for Paradise.
tafsir: Allah purchased your life and wealth in exchange for Paradise. The best transaction ever.
reflectionTemplate: Your life and wealth are a transaction with Allah. The price: Paradise. The best deal you will ever make.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking purpose. Allah purchased your life and wealth in exchange for Paradise.

arabic: لِّلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنَىٰ وَزِيَادَةٌ
translation: For those who have done good is the best reward and extra.
tafsir: Those who do good get the best reward — and MORE. Allah's generosity exceeds expectations.
reflectionTemplate: Your good deeds bring the best reward — and extra. Allah's generosity exceeds your expectations.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone wondering if good deeds matter. Those who do good get the best reward — and extra.

arabic: أَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
translation: Unquestionably, for the allies of Allah there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
tafsir: Allah's allies have no fear or grief. The path: faith + taqwa.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa makes you Allah's ally. No fear, no grief — keep your taqwa strong.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone fearing the future. Allah's allies have no fear — faith + taqwa.

arabic: وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِّنَ اللَّيْلِ ۚ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَاتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئَاتِ
translation: And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds remove bad deeds.
tafsir: The five prayers erase sins. Each salah wipes away the sins before it.
reflectionTemplate: Your prayer consistency needs work. Each salah erases sins — do not miss this mercy.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone feeling weighed down by sins. The solution: the five daily prayers erase sins.

arabic: وَالَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا ابْتِغَاءَ وَجْهِ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ
translation: And those who are patient, seeking the countenance of their Lord, and establish prayer.
tafsir: The patient ones seek Allah's countenance and establish prayer. Patience + prayer = seeking Allah's face.
reflectionTemplate: Your patience has a purpose: seeking Allah's countenance. Combine patience with prayer.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone in hardship. Patience + prayer = seeking Allah's countenance.

arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً كَشَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ أَصْلُهَا ثَابِتٌ وَفَرْعُهَا فِي السَّمَاءِ تُؤْتِي أُكُلَهَا كُلَّ حِينٍ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهَا
translation: Have you not considered how Allah presents an example? A good word is like a good tree, whose root is firmly fixed and its branches in the sky. It produces its fruit all the time by permission of its Lord.
tafsir: A good word (faith) is like a tree — firmly rooted, branches in heaven, always producing fruit.
reflectionTemplate: Your faith is like a tree — rooted firmly, branches in heaven, always producing fruit. Keep it strong.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking lasting impact. Faith is like a tree — rooted firmly, always producing fruit.

arabic: إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوا وَّالَّذِينَ هُم مُّحْسِنُونَ
translation: Indeed, Allah is with those who fear Him and those who are doers of good.
tafsir: Allah is with those who have taqwa and do good. Fear Him + do good = Allah is with you.
reflectionTemplate: Your companion is Allah Himself. Fear Him and do good — and He is with you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking Allah's company. Fear Him and do good — and Allah is with you.

arabic: وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِ نَافِلَةً لَّكَ عَسَىٰ أَن يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقَامًا مَّحْمُودًا
translation: And from part of the night, pray with it as additional worship for you; it is expected that your Lord will resurrect you to a praised station.
tafsir: Tahajjud leads to the praised station (Maqam Mahmud) on Judgment Day. Night prayer elevates your rank.
reflectionTemplate: Your spiritual rank needs tahajjud. Night prayer leads to the praised station — wake up for it.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking high rank. Tahajjud leads to the praised station — wake up for it.

arabic: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
translation: And We have not sent you, O Muhammad, except as a mercy to the worlds.
tafsir: The Prophet ﷺ was sent as mercy — not punishment. Islam is mercy to all creation.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah approach needs mercy. The Prophet ﷺ was mercy — your dawah should be mercy too.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks Islam is harsh. The Prophet ﷺ was sent as mercy to all worlds.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ارْكَعُوا وَاسْجُدُوا وَاعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمْ وَافْعَلُوا الْخَيْرَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
translation: O you who have believed, bow and prostrate and worship your Lord and do good that you may succeed.
tafsir: Success comes from worship AND doing good — faith and action together.
reflectionTemplate: Your success needs worship AND action. Bow, prostrate, worship, and do good.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who knows but does not act. Knowledge without action is incomplete.

arabic: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي صَلَاتِهِمْ خَاشِعُونَ
translation: Certainly will the believers have succeeded: They who are during their prayer humbly submissive.
tafsir: Success is defined by khushu in prayer — not just movements but a humble, present heart.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is low — your prayers may lack khushu. Focus on being present in your next salah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who thinks prayer is just movements. True success is in the humility of the heart.

arabic: وَإِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلًا صَالِحًا فَأُولَٰئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ اللَّهُ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ حَسَنَاتٍ
translation: Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work — for them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds.
tafsir: Allah replaces evil deeds with good deeds for those who repent and believe. Your past sins can become good deeds.
reflectionTemplate: Your past mistakes can become good deeds. Repent, believe, do good — Allah transforms your sins into rewards.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone ashamed of their past. Allah replaces evil with good for those who repent.

arabic: وَالَّذِينَ جَاهَدُوا فِينَا لَنَهْدِيَنَّهُمْ سُبُلَنَا
translation: And those who strive for Us — We will surely guide them to Our ways.
tafsir: Strive in Allah's way — and He will guide you to His paths. Guidance comes through striving.
reflectionTemplate: Your guidance comes through striving. Strive in Allah's way — He will guide you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking guidance. Strive in Allah's way — He will guide you to His paths.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ
translation: O you who have believed, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice. He will amend for you your deeds.
tafsir: Taqwa + truthful speech = reformed deeds. What you say reflects your God-consciousness.
reflectionTemplate: Your speech needs this standard. Fear Allah and speak truthfully — He will fix your deeds.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with speech. Fear Allah and speak truth — He will fix everything else.

arabic: قُلْ يَا عِبَادِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا فِي هَٰذِهِ الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ
translation: Say: O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good.
tafsir: Fear Allah and do good — good in this world and the next.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa is the foundation. Fear Allah and do good — good will come to you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking a good life. Fear Allah and do good — good will come to you.

arabic: تَرَى الظَّالِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا وَهُوَ وَاقِعٌ بِهِمْ
translation: You will see the wrongdoers fearful of what they have earned, and it will befall them.
tafsir: The wrongdoers will face what they earned. Justice is guaranteed — if not in this world, then in the hereafter.
reflectionTemplate: Your qadr perception needs this truth. The wrongdoers will face justice — Allah's justice is certain.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone questioning injustice. The wrongdoers will face what they earned — justice is guaranteed.

arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
translation: Indeed, those who have said, 'Our Lord is Allah' and then remained on a right course — there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
tafsir: Steadfastness after declaring faith brings ultimate peace — no fear, no grief.
reflectionTemplate: Your strong streak shows your steadfastness. Continue — no fear, no grief awaits those who remain firm.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone who is steadfast. The reward: no fear, no grief. Continue on this path.

arabic: مُّحَمَّدٌ رَّسُولُ اللَّهِ ۚ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ أَشِدَّاءُ عَلَى الْكُفَّارِ رُحَمَاءُ بَيْنَهُمْ
translation: Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah; and those with him are forceful against the disbelievers, merciful among themselves.
tafsir: The believers: strong against falsehood, merciful among themselves. Balance of strength and mercy.
reflectionTemplate: Your dawah needs this balance. Be strong against falsehood, merciful among believers.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking the believer's character. Strong against falsehood, merciful among believers.

arabic: فَبِأَيِّ آلَاءِ رَبِّكُمَا تُكَذِّبَانِ
translation: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
tafsir: Allah asks repeatedly: which of His favors will you deny? Every blessing is from Him.
reflectionTemplate: Your gratitude needs this question. Which of Allah's favors will you deny? Be grateful.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone ungrateful. Which of Allah's favors will you deny? Be grateful.

arabic: اعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَزِينَةٌ وَتَفَاخُرٌ بَيْنَكُمْ وَتَكَاثُرٌ فِي الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَوْلَادِ
translation: Know that the life of this world is but amusement and diversion and adornment and boasting to one another and competition in increase of wealth and children.
tafsir: Dunya is play, decoration, and competition. The real life is the akhirah.
reflectionTemplate: You are competing in dunya. Remember: this is play. The real competition is for Jannah.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone consumed by worldly competition. Allah calls it 'play and diversion.'

arabic: أُولَٰئِكَ كَتَبَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْإِيمَانَ وَأَيَّدَهُم بِرُوحٍ مِّنْهُ
translation: Those are the ones in whose hearts Allah has inscribed faith and supported them with a spirit from Him.
tafsir: Allah Himself writes faith in the hearts of true believers and supports them with divine help.
reflectionTemplate: Your faith is a gift from Allah — He wrote it in your heart. Protect it and He will support you.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone doubting their faith. If you believe, Allah Himself wrote that faith in your heart.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قُوا أَنفُسَكُمْ وَأَهْلِيكُمْ نَارًا
translation: O you who have believed, protect yourselves and your families from a Fire.
tafsir: Protect yourself and your family from Hellfire. Your responsibility extends to your family.
reflectionTemplate: Your social deen includes protecting your family. Guide them to good and protect them from the Fire.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone neglecting their family's guidance. Protect yourself and your family from the Fire.

arabic: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَامْشُوا فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا مِن رِّزْقِهِ
translation: It is He who made the earth tame for you, so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision.
tafsir: The earth is made for you — walk, explore, eat from Allah's provision.
reflectionTemplate: Your health barakah is low but the earth is made for you. Walk, move, explore.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone trapped by health issues. The earth is made for you — walk and eat from His provision.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الْمُزَّمِّلُ قُمِ اللَّيْلَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا نِّصْفَهُ أَوِ انقُصْ مِنْهُ قَلِيلًا أَوْ زِدْ عَلَيْهِ وَرَتِّلِ الْقُرْآنَ تَرْتِيلًا
translation: O you who wraps himself, arise to pray at night, except for a little — half of it or slightly less, or add to it, and recite the Quran with measured recitation.
tafsir: Tahajjud is the prayer of the closest servants to Allah. Even a little night worship transforms the soul.
reflectionTemplate: Your heart is restless. The cure is in tahajjud — even two rak'ahs can change everything.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone spiritually disconnected. The night prayer is the secret weapon of the believers.

arabic: إِنَّ الْأَبْرَارَ يَشْرَبُونَ مِن كَأْسٍ كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا كَافُورًا عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ اللَّهِ يُفَجِّرُونَهَا تَفْجِيرًا
translation: Indeed, the righteous will drink from a cup whose mixture is of Kafur, a spring from which the servants of Allah will drink.
tafsir: The reward of the righteous in Jannah is beyond imagination — rivers of pure drink flowing eternally.
reflectionTemplate: Your akhirah orientation needs this vision. The reward in Jannah is beyond anything this world can offer.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone attached to worldly pleasures. The real enjoyment is in the hereafter.

arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنسَانُ مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ الَّذِي خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّاكَ فَعَدَلَكَ
translation: O mankind, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous, who created you, proportioned you, and balanced you?
tafsir: Allah asks what deceived you about His generosity. He created you perfectly — trust His care.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah perception is distorted. He created you perfectly — trust His care.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone doubting Allah's care. He created you perfectly — what deceived you?

arabic: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ وَاتَّقَىٰ وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنَىٰ فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرَىٰ
translation: As for he who gives and fears Allah and believes in the best reward, We will ease him toward ease.
tafsir: Generosity + taqwa + belief in good = ease from Allah.
reflectionTemplate: Your barakah is connected to your generosity and taqwa. Give, fear Allah, and believe — ease will come.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone struggling with barakah. The formula: give, fear Allah, believe — ease follows.

arabic: لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنسَانَ فِي أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَاهُ أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ
translation: We have certainly created man in the best of stature. Then We return him to the lowest of the low. Except for those who believe and do righteous deeds.
tafsir: Humans were created in the best form — but can fall. Except believers who do good.
reflectionTemplate: Your honor depends on faith and action. Maintain your honor through belief and good deeds.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone losing their way. Created in the best form — maintain it through faith and good deeds.

arabic: اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ خَلَقَ الْإِنسَانَ مِنْ عَلَقٍ اقْرَأْ وَرَبُّكَ الْأَكْرَمُ الَّذِي عَلَّمَ بِالْقَلَمِ عَلَّمَ الْإِنسَانَ مَا لَمْ يَعْلَمْ
translation: Read in the name of your Lord who created. Created man from a clinging substance. Read, and your Lord is the Most Generous. Who taught by the pen. Taught man that which he knew not.
tafsir: The first revelation: read! And your Lord is the Most Generous. Knowledge is a divine gift.
reflectionTemplate: Your ilm journey starts here. Read in the name of your Lord — the Most Generous.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone seeking knowledge. Read in the name of your Lord — the Most Generous.

arabic: وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ حُنَفَاءَ وَيُقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَيُؤْتُوا الزَّكَاةَ
translation: And they were not commanded except to worship Allah, being sincere to Him in religion, inclining to truth, and to establish prayer and give zakah.
tafsir: The correct religion: sincere worship + prayer + zakah. Simplicity itself.
reflectionTemplate: Your taqwa needs this clarity. Sincere worship + prayer + zakah = the correct religion.
dawahTemplate: Share this with someone overwhelmed by Islam. The basics: worship Allah sincerely, pray, give zakah.
